This is documentation for Tabsdata 0.9.1, which is no longer actively maintained.
For up-to-date documentation, see the latest version (2.0.0).
  • TableFrame API
Version: 0.9.1

TableFrame API

TableFrame​

TableFrame
class TableFrame(
    df: Mapping [ str , Sequence [ object ] | Mapping [ str , Sequence [ object ] ] ] | TableFrame | None = None,
)

Bases: object

Owned Functions.

cast​
def cast(
    dtypes: Mapping [ ColumnNameOrSelector | td_typing.TdDataType , td_typing.TdDataType ] | td_typing.TdDataType,
    *,
    strict: bool = True,
) -> TableFrame

View source ↗

Cast columns to a new data type.

Parameters:

dtypesMapping [ ColumnNameOrSelector | td_typing.TdDataType , td_typing.TdDataType ] | td_typing.TdDataTyperequired

Mapping of the column name(s) to the new data type(s).

strictbooldefault: True

IfTrue, raises an error if the cast cannot be performed.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ a    ┆ b    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ A    ┆ 1    │
│ X    ┆ 10   │
│ C    ┆ 3    │
│ D    ┆ 5    │
│ M    ┆ 9    │
│ A    ┆ 100  │
│ M    ┆ 50   │
│ null ┆ 20   │
│ F    ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.cast({"b":pl.Float32}).collect()
>>>
┌──────┬───────┐
│ a    ┆ b     │
│ ---  ┆ ---   │
│ str  ┆ f32   │
╞══════╪═══════╡
│ A    ┆ 1.0   │
│ X    ┆ 10.0  │
│ C    ┆ 3.0   │
│ D    ┆ 5.0   │
│ M    ┆ 9.0   │
│ A    ┆ 100.0 │
│ M    ┆ 50.0  │
│ null ┆ 20.0  │
│ F    ┆ null  │
└──────┴───────┘
clear​
def clear(
    n: int = 0,
) -> TableFrame

View source ↗

Clears all rows of the TableFrame preserving the schema.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ a    ┆ b    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ A    ┆ 1    │
│ X    ┆ 10   │
│ C    ┆ 3    │
│ D    ┆ 5    │
│ M    ┆ 9    │
│ A    ┆ 100  │
│ M    ┆ 50   │
│ null ┆ 20   │
│ F    ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.cast({"b":pl.Float32}).collect()
>>>
┌──────┬───────┐
│ a    ┆ b     │
│ ---  ┆ ---   │
│ str  ┆ f32   │
╞══════╪═══════╡
└──────┴───────┘
drop​
def drop(
    * columns: ColumnNameOrSelector | Iterable [ ColumnNameOrSelector ],
    strict: bool = True,
) -> TableFrame

View source ↗

Discard columns from the TableFrame.

Parameters:

columnsrequired

Columns to drop.

strictbooldefault: True

If True, raises an error if a column does not exist.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ x    ┆ y    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ f64  ┆ f64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ 1.0  ┆ 2.0  │
│ 2.0  ┆ 2.0  │
│ 10.0 ┆ 10.0 │
│ 4.0  ┆ 10.0 │
│ 5.0  ┆ null │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.drop("y")
>>>
┌──────┐
│ x    │
│ ---  │
│ f64  │
╞══════╡
│ 1.0  │
│ 2.0  │
│ NaN  │
│ 4.0  │
│ 5.0  │
│ null │
└──────┘
drop_nans​
def drop_nans(
    subset: ColumnNameOrSelector | Collection [ ColumnNameOrSelector ] | None = None,
) -> TableFrame

View source ↗

Drop rows with NaN values.

Parameters:

subsetColumnNameOrSelector | Collection [ ColumnNameOrSelector ] | Nonedefault: None

Columns to look forNan values. If None, all columns are considered.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┬──────┐
│ ss   ┆ u    ┆ ff   │
│ ---  ┆ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  ┆ f64  │
╞══════╪══════╪══════╡
│ A    ┆ 1    ┆ 1.1  │
│ B    ┆ 0    ┆ 0.0  │
│ A    ┆ 2    ┆ 2.2  │
│ B    ┆ 3    ┆ 3.3  │
│ B    ┆ 4    ┆ 4.4  │
│ C    ┆ 5    ┆ -1.1 │
│ C    ┆ 6    ┆ -2.2 │
│ C    ┆ 7    ┆ -3.3 │
│ D    ┆ 8    ┆ inf  │
│ F    ┆ 9    ┆ NaN  │
│ null ┆ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.unique("a", keep="last")
┌─────┬─────┬──────┐
│ ss  ┆ u   ┆ ff   │
│ --- ┆ --- ┆ ---  │
│ str ┆ i64 ┆ f64  │
╞═════╪═════╪══════╡
│ A   ┆ 1   ┆ 1.1  │
│ B   ┆ 0   ┆ 0.0  │
│ A   ┆ 2   ┆ 2.2  │
│ B   ┆ 3   ┆ 3.3  │
│ B   ┆ 4   ┆ 4.4  │
│ C   ┆ 5   ┆ -1.1 │
│ C   ┆ 6   ┆ -2.2 │
│ C   ┆ 7   ┆ -3.3 │
│ D   ┆ 8   ┆ inf  │
└─────┴─────┴──────┘
>>>
drop_nulls​
def drop_nulls(
    subset: ColumnNameOrSelector | Collection [ ColumnNameOrSelector ] | None = None,
) -> TableFrame

View source ↗

Drop rows with null values.

Parameters:

subsetColumnNameOrSelector | Collection [ ColumnNameOrSelector ] | Nonedefault: None

Columns to evaluate for null values. If None, all columns are considered.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┬──────┐
│ ss   ┆ u    ┆ ff   │
│ ---  ┆ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  ┆ f64  │
╞══════╪══════╪══════╡
│ A    ┆ 1    ┆ 1.1  │
│ B    ┆ 0    ┆ 0.0  │
│ A    ┆ 2    ┆ 2.2  │
│ B    ┆ 3    ┆ 3.3  │
│ B    ┆ 4    ┆ 4.4  │
│ C    ┆ 5    ┆ -1.1 │
│ C    ┆ 6    ┆ -2.2 │
│ C    ┆ 7    ┆ -3.3 │
│ D    ┆ 8    ┆ inf  │
│ F    ┆ 9    ┆ NaN  │
│ G    ┆ null ┆ 2.3  │
└──────┴──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.drop_nulls("a")
>>>
┌─────┬─────┬──────┐
│ ss  ┆ u   ┆ ff   │
│ --- ┆ --- ┆ ---  │
│ str ┆ i64 ┆ f64  │
╞═════╪═════╪══════╡
│ A   ┆ 1   ┆ 1.1  │
│ B   ┆ 0   ┆ 0.0  │
│ A   ┆ 2   ┆ 2.2  │
│ B   ┆ 3   ┆ 3.3  │
│ B   ┆ 4   ┆ 4.4  │
│ C   ┆ 5   ┆ -1.1 │
│ C   ┆ 6   ┆ -2.2 │
│ C   ┆ 7   ┆ -3.3 │
│ D   ┆ 8   ┆ inf  │
│ F   ┆ 9   ┆ NaN  │
└─────┴─────┴──────┘
empty​
def empty() -> TableFrame

View source ↗

explain​
def explain(
    ** kwargs,
)

View source ↗

Provide private accessibility level wrapper.

fill_nan​
def fill_nan(
    value: int | float | Expr | None,
) -> TableFrame

View source ↗

Replace all NaN values in the TableFrame with the given value.

Parameters:

valueint | float | Expr | Nonerequired

The value to replaceNaN with.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ x    ┆ y    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ f64  ┆ f64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ 1.0  ┆ 2.0  │
│ 2.0  ┆ 2.0  │
│ NaN  ┆ NaN  │
│ 4.0  ┆ NaN  │
│ 5.0  ┆ null │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.fill_nan(10)
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ x    ┆ y    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ f64  ┆ f64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ 1.0  ┆ 2.0  │
│ 2.0  ┆ 2.0  │
│ 10.0 ┆ 10.0 │
│ 4.0  ┆ 10.0 │
│ 5.0  ┆ null │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
fill_null​
def fill_null(
    value: Any | Expr | None = None,
) -> TableFrame

View source ↗

Replace all null values in the TableFrame with the given value.

Parameters:

valueAny | Expr | Nonedefault: None

The value to replacenull with.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ x    ┆ y    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ f64  ┆ f64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ 1.0  ┆ 2.0  │
│ 2.0  ┆ 2.0  │
│ NaN  ┆ NaN  │
│ 4.0  ┆ NaN  │
│ 5.0  ┆ null │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.fill_null(20)
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ x    ┆ y    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ f64  ┆ f64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ 1.0  ┆ 2.0  │
│ 2.0  ┆ 2.0  │
│ NaN  ┆ NaN  │
│ 4.0  ┆ NaN  │
│ 5.0  ┆ 20.0 │
│ 20.0 ┆ 20.0 │
└──────┴──────┘
filter​
def filter(
    * predicates: Expr | Series | str | Iterable [ Expr | Series | str ] | bool | list [ bool ] | ndarray [ Any , Any ],
) -> TableFrame

View source ↗

Filter the TableFrame based on the given predicates.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌─────┬─────┐
│ a   ┆ b   │
│ --- ┆ --- │
│ str ┆ i64 │
╞═════╪═════╡
│ A   ┆ 1   │
│ X   ┆ 10  │
│ C   ┆ 3   │
│ D   ┆ 5   │
│ M   ┆ 9   │
│ A   ┆ 100 │
│ M   ┆ 50  │
└─────┴─────┘
>>>
>>> tf.filter(td.col("a").is_in(["A", "C"]).or_(td.col("b").eq(10)))
>>>
┌─────┬─────┐
│ a   ┆ b   │
│ --- ┆ --- │
│ str ┆ i64 │
╞═════╪═════╡
│ A   ┆ 1   │
│ X   ┆ 10  │
│ C   ┆ 3   │
│ A   ┆ 100 │
└─────┴─────┘
first​
def first() -> TableFrame

View source ↗

Return a TableFrame with the first row.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌─────┬─────┐
│ a   ┆ b   │
│ --- ┆ --- │
│ str ┆ i64 │
╞═════╪═════╡
│ A   ┆ 1   │
│ X   ┆ 10  │
│ C   ┆ 3   │
│ D   ┆ 5   │
│ M   ┆ 9   │
└─────┴─────┘
>>>
>>> tf.first()
>>>
┌─────┬─────┐
│ a   ┆ b   │
│ --- ┆ --- │
│ str ┆ i64 │
╞═════╪═════╡
│ A   ┆ 1   │
└─────┴─────┘
group_by​
def group_by(
    * by: td_expr.IntoExpr | Iterable [ td_expr.IntoExpr ],
) -> td_group_by.TableFrameGroupBy

View source ↗

Perform a group by on the TableFrame.

Parameters:

byrequired

Columns or expressions to group by.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌─────┬─────┐
│ a   ┆ b   │
│ --- ┆ --- │
│ str ┆ i64 │
╞═════╪═════╡
│ A   ┆ 1   │
│ X   ┆ 10  │
│ C   ┆ 3   │
│ D   ┆ 5   │
│ M   ┆ 9   │
│ A   ┆ 100 │
│ M   ┆ 50  │
└─────┴─────┘
>>>
>>> tf.group_by(td.col("a")).agg(td.col("b").sum())
>>>
┌─────┬─────┐
│ a   ┆ b   │
│ --- ┆ --- │
│ str ┆ i64 │
╞═════╪═════╡
│ M   ┆ 59  │
│ A   ┆ 101 │
│ C   ┆ 3   │
│ D   ┆ 5   │
│ X   ┆ 10  │
└─────┴─────┘
head​
def head(
    n: int = 5,
) -> TableFrame

View source ↗

Return a TableFrame with the first n rows.

Parameters:

nintdefault: 5

The number of rows to return.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌─────┬─────┐
│ a   ┆ b   │
│ --- ┆ --- │
│ str ┆ i64 │
╞═════╪═════╡
│ A   ┆ 1   │
│ X   ┆ 10  │
│ C   ┆ 3   │
│ D   ┆ 5   │
│ M   ┆ 9   │
└─────┴─────┘
>>>
>>> tf.head(2)
>>>
┌─────┬─────┐
│ a   ┆ b   │
│ --- ┆ --- │
│ str ┆ i64 │
╞═════╪═════╡
│ A   ┆ 1   │
│ X   ┆ 10  │
└─────┴─────┘
inspect​
def inspect(
    ** kwargs,
)

View source ↗

Provide private accessibility level wrapper.

item​
def item() -> Any

View source ↗

join​
def join(
    other: TableFrame,
    on: str | Expr | Sequence [ str | Expr ] | None = None,
    how: Literal [ 'inner' , 'left' , 'right' , 'full' , 'semi' , 'anti' , 'cross' , 'outer' ] = 'inner',
    *,
    left_on: str | Expr | Sequence [ str | Expr ] | None = None,
    right_on: str | Expr | Sequence [ str | Expr ] | None = None,
    suffix: str = '_right',
    join_nulls: bool = False,
    coalesce: bool | None = None,
) -> TableFrame

View source ↗

Join the TableFrame with another TableFrame.

Parameters:

otherTableFramerequired

TheTableFrame to join.

onstr | Expr | Sequence [ str | Expr ] | Nonedefault: None

Name(s) of the columns to join on. The column name(s) must be in bothTableFrame’s. Don’t use this parameter if using `left_on and right_on parameters, or if how=”cross”.

howLiteral [ 'inner' , 'left' , 'right' , 'full' , 'semi' , 'anti' , 'cross' , 'outer' ]default: 'inner'

Join strategy: * inner: An inner join. * left: A left join. * right: A rigth join. * full: A full join. * cross: The cartesian product. * semi: An inner join but only returning the columns from left TableFrame. anti: Rows from the left TableFrame that have no matchin the right TableFrame.

left_onstr | Expr | Sequence [ str | Expr ] | Nonedefault: None

Name(s) of the columns to join on from the leftTableFrame. It must be used together wit the right_on parameter. It cannot be used with the on parameter.

right_onstr | Expr | Sequence [ str | Expr ] | Nonedefault: None

Name(s) of the columns to join on from the rightTableFrame. It must be used together wit the left_on parameter. It cannot be used with the on parameter.

suffixstrdefault: '_right'

Duplicate columns on the rightTable are appended this suffix.

join_nullsbooldefault: False

Ifnull value matches should produce join rows or not.

coalescebool | Nonedefault: None

Collapse join columns into a single column.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf1: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ a    ┆ b    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ A    ┆ 1    │
│ X    ┆ 10   │
│ C    ┆ 3    │
│ D    ┆ 5    │
│ M    ┆ 9    │
│ A    ┆ 100  │
│ M    ┆ 50   │
│ null ┆ 20   │
│ F    ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf2: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ a    ┆ b    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ A    ┆ 3    │
│ Y    ┆ 4    │
│ Z    ┆ 5    │
│ A    ┆ 0    │
│ M    ┆ 6    │
│ null ┆ 8    │
│ F    ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
>>>
An inner join:
>>>
>>> tf1.join(tf2, on="a", how="inner")
>>>
┌─────┬──────┬─────────┐
│ a   ┆ b    ┆ b_right │
│ --- ┆ ---  ┆ ---     │
│ str ┆ i64  ┆ i64     │
╞═════╪══════╪═════════╡
│ A   ┆ 1    ┆ 3       │
│ A   ┆ 1    ┆ 0       │
│ M   ┆ 9    ┆ 6       │
│ A   ┆ 100  ┆ 3       │
│ A   ┆ 100  ┆ 0       │
│ M   ┆ 50   ┆ 6       │
│ F   ┆ null ┆ null    │
└─────┴──────┴─────────┘
>>>
A left join:
>>>
>>> tf1.join(tf2, on="a", how="left")
>>>
┌──────┬──────┬─────────┐
│ a    ┆ b    ┆ b_right │
│ ---  ┆ ---  ┆ ---     │
│ str  ┆ i64  ┆ i64     │
╞══════╪══════╪═════════╡
│ A    ┆ 1    ┆ 3       │
│ A    ┆ 1    ┆ 0       │
│ X    ┆ 10   ┆ null    │
│ C    ┆ 3    ┆ null    │
│ D    ┆ 5    ┆ null    │
│ …    ┆ …    ┆ …       │
│ A    ┆ 100  ┆ 3       │
│ A    ┆ 100  ┆ 0       │
│ M    ┆ 50   ┆ 6       │
│ null ┆ 20   ┆ null    │
│ F    ┆ null ┆ null    │
└──────┴──────┴─────────┘
>>>
Turning off column coalesce:
>>>
>>> tf1.join(tf2, on="a", coalesce=False)
>>>
┌─────┬──────┬─────────┬─────────┐
│ a   ┆ b    ┆ a_right ┆ b_right │
│ --- ┆ ---  ┆ ---     ┆ ---     │
│ str ┆ i64  ┆ str     ┆ i64     │
╞═════╪══════╪═════════╪═════════╡
│ A   ┆ 1    ┆ A       ┆ 3       │
│ A   ┆ 1    ┆ A       ┆ 0       │
│ M   ┆ 9    ┆ M       ┆ 6       │
│ A   ┆ 100  ┆ A       ┆ 3       │
│ A   ┆ 100  ┆ A       ┆ 0       │
│ M   ┆ 50   ┆ M       ┆ 6       │
│ F   ┆ null ┆ F       ┆ null    │
└─────┴──────┴─────────┴─────────┘
last​
def last() -> TableFrame

View source ↗

Return a TableFrame with the last row.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌─────┬─────┐
│ a   ┆ b   │
│ --- ┆ --- │
│ str ┆ i64 │
╞═════╪═════╡
│ A   ┆ 1   │
│ X   ┆ 10  │
│ C   ┆ 3   │
│ D   ┆ 5   │
│ M   ┆ 9   │
└─────┴─────┘
>>>
>>> tf.last()
>>>
┌─────┬─────┐
│ a   ┆ b   │
│ --- ┆ --- │
│ str ┆ i64 │
╞═════╪═════╡
│ M   ┆ 9   │
└─────┴─────┘
limit​
def limit(
    n: int = 5,
) -> TableFrame

View source ↗

Return a TableFrame with the first n rows. This is equivalent to head.

Parameters:

nintdefault: 5

The number of rows to return.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌─────┬─────┐
│ a   ┆ b   │
│ --- ┆ --- │
│ str ┆ i64 │
╞═════╪═════╡
│ A   ┆ 1   │
│ X   ┆ 10  │
│ C   ┆ 3   │
│ D   ┆ 5   │
│ M   ┆ 9   │
└─────┴─────┘
>>>
>>> tf.limit(2)
>>>
┌─────┬─────┐
│ a   ┆ b   │
│ --- ┆ --- │
│ str ┆ i64 │
╞═════╪═════╡
│ A   ┆ 1   │
│ X   ┆ 10  │
└─────┴─────┘
select​
def select(
    * exprs: td_expr.IntoExpr | Iterable [ td_expr.IntoExpr ],
    ** named_exprs: td_expr.IntoExpr,
) -> TableFrame

View source ↗

Select column(s) from the TableFrame.

Parameters:

exprsrequired

Columns or expressions to select.

named_exprsrequired

Named expressions to select.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌─────┬─────┐
│ a   ┆ b   │
│ --- ┆ --- │
│ str ┆ i64 │
╞═════╪═════╡
│ A   ┆ 1   │
│ X   ┆ 10  │
│ C   ┆ 3   │
│ D   ┆ 5   │
│ M   ┆ 9   │
│ A   ┆ 100 │
│ M   ┆ 50  │
└─────┴─────┘
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("a"), td.col("b").mul(2).alias("bx2"),)
>>>
┌─────┬─────┐
│ a   ┆ bx2 │
│ --- ┆ --- │
│ str ┆ i64 │
╞═════╪═════╡
│ A   ┆ 2   │
│ X   ┆ 20  │
│ C   ┆ 6   │
│ D   ┆ 10  │
│ M   ┆ 18  │
│ A   ┆ 200 │
│ M   ┆ 100 │
└─────┴─────┘
show_graph​
def show_graph(
    ** kwargs,
)

View source ↗

Provide private accessibility level wrapper.

slice​
def slice(
    offset: int,
    length: int | None = None,
) -> TableFrame

View source ↗

Return a TableFrame with a slice of the original TableFrame

Parameters:

offsetintrequired

Slice starting index.

lengthint | Nonedefault: None

The length of the slice.None means all the way to the end.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌─────┬─────┐
│ a   ┆ b   │
│ --- ┆ --- │
│ str ┆ i64 │
╞═════╪═════╡
│ A   ┆ 1   │
│ X   ┆ 10  │
│ C   ┆ 3   │
│ D   ┆ 5   │
│ M   ┆ 9   │
└─────┴─────┘
>>>
>>> tf.slice(2,2)
>>>
┌─────┬─────┐
│ a   ┆ b   │
│ --- ┆ --- │
│ str ┆ i64 │
╞═════╪═════╡
│ C   ┆ 3   │
│ D   ┆ 5   │
└─────┴─────┘
sort​
def sort(
    by: td_expr.IntoExpr | Iterable [ td_expr.IntoExpr ],
    * more_by: td_expr.IntoExpr,
    descending: bool | Sequence [ bool ] = False,
    nulls_last: bool | Sequence [ bool ] = False,
    maintain_order: bool = False,
) -> TableFrame

View source ↗

Sort the TableFrame by the given column(s) or expression(s).

Parameters:

bytd_expr.IntoExpr | Iterable [ td_expr.IntoExpr ]required

Column(s) or expression(s) to sort by.

more_byrequired

Additional colums to sort by.

descendingbool | Sequence [ bool ]default: False

Specifies if the sorting should be descending or not.

nulls_lastbool | Sequence [ bool ]default: False

Specifies ifnull values should be placed last.

maintain_orderbooldefault: False

Preserve the order of equal rows.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ a    ┆ b    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ A    ┆ 1    │
│ X    ┆ 10   │
│ C    ┆ 3    │
│ D    ┆ 5    │
│ M    ┆ 9    │
│ A    ┆ 100  │
│ M    ┆ 50   │
│ null ┆ 20   │
│ F    ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.sort(td.col("a"), descending = True)
>>>
┌──────┬───────┐
│ a    ┆ b     │
│ ---  ┆ ---   │
│ str  ┆ f32   │
╞══════╪═══════╡
│ A    ┆ 1.0   │
│ X    ┆ 10.0  │
│ C    ┆ 3.0   │
│ D    ┆ 5.0   │
│ M    ┆ 9.0   │
│ A    ┆ 100.0 │
│ M    ┆ 50.0  │
│ null ┆ 20.0  │
│ F    ┆ null  │
└──────┴───────┘
tail​
def tail(
    n: int = 5,
) -> TableFrame

View source ↗

Return a TableFrame with the last n rows.

Parameters:

nintdefault: 5

The number of rows to return.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌─────┬─────┐
│ a   ┆ b   │
│ --- ┆ --- │
│ str ┆ i64 │
╞═════╪═════╡
│ A   ┆ 1   │
│ X   ┆ 10  │
│ C   ┆ 3   │
│ D   ┆ 5   │
│ M   ┆ 9   │
└─────┴─────┘
>>>
>>> tf.tail(2)
>>>
┌─────┬─────┐
│ a   ┆ b   │
│ --- ┆ --- │
│ str ┆ i64 │
╞═════╪═════╡
│ D   ┆ 5   │
│ M   ┆ 9   │
└─────┴─────┘
unique​
def unique(
    subset: ColumnNameOrSelector | Collection [ ColumnNameOrSelector ] | None = None,
    *,
    keep: UniqueKeepStrategy = 'any',
    maintain_order: bool = False,
) -> TableFrame

View source ↗

Deduplicate rows from the TableFrame.

Parameters:

subsetColumnNameOrSelector | Collection [ ColumnNameOrSelector ] | Nonedefault: None

Columns to evaluate for duplicate values. If None, all columns are considered.

keepUniqueKeepStrategydefault: 'any'

Strategy to keep duplicates:first, last, any, none ( eliminate duplicate rows).

maintain_orderbooldefault: False

Preserve the order of the rows.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ a    ┆ b    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ A    ┆ 1    │
│ X    ┆ 10   │
│ C    ┆ 3    │
│ D    ┆ 5    │
│ M    ┆ 9    │
│ A    ┆ 100  │
│ M    ┆ 50   │
│ null ┆ 20   │
│ F    ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.unique("a", keep="last")
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ a    ┆ b    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ D    ┆ 5    │
│ C    ┆ 3    │
│ X    ┆ 10   │
│ A    ┆ 100  │
│ M    ┆ 50   │
│ F    ┆ null │
│ null ┆ 20   │
└──────┴──────┘
with_columns​
def with_columns(
    * exprs: td_expr.IntoExpr | Iterable [ td_expr.IntoExpr ],
    ** named_exprs: td_expr.IntoExpr,
) -> TableFrame

View source ↗

Add columns to the TableFrame.

Parameters:

exprsrequired

Columns or expressions to add.

named_exprsrequired

Named expressions to add.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ x    ┆ y    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ f64  ┆ f64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ 1.0  ┆ 2.0  │
│ 2.0  ┆ 2.0  │
│ 10.0 ┆ 10.0 │
│ 4.0  ┆ 10.0 │
│ 5.0  ┆ null │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.with_columns(td.col("x").mul(td.col("y")).alias("z"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────┬──────┐
│ x    ┆ y    ┆ z    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ f64  ┆ f64  ┆ f64  │
╞══════╪══════╪══════╡
│ 1.0  ┆ 2.0  ┆ 2.0  │
│ 2.0  ┆ 2.0  ┆ 4.0  │
│ NaN  ┆ NaN  ┆ NaN  │
│ 4.0  ┆ NaN  ┆ NaN  │
│ 5.0  ┆ null ┆ null │
│ null ┆ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┴──────┘
columnslist [ str ] #
dtypeslist [ DataType ] #
schemaSchema #
widthint #

GroupBy​

TableFrameGroupBy
class TableFrameGroupBy(
    lgb: LazyGroupBy | TableFrameGroupBy,
)

View source ↗

Bases: object

agg​
def agg(
    * aggs: td_expr.IntoExpr | Iterable [ td_expr.IntoExpr ],
    ** named_aggs: td_expr.IntoExpr,
) -> td_frame.TableFrame

View source ↗

Aggregation expressions for the group by column(s).

Parameters:

*aggsrequired

Aggregation operations.

Examples​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ a    ┆ b    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ A    ┆ 1    │
│ B    ┆ 2    │
│ A    ┆ 3    │
│ B    ┆ 0    │
│ C    ┆ 5    │
│ null ┆ 6    │
│ C    ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.group_by(td.col("a")).agg(td.col("b").sum().alias("agg")).collect()
>>>
┌──────┬─────┐
│ a    ┆ agg │
│ ---  ┆ --- │
│ str  ┆ i64 │
╞══════╪═════╡
│ A    ┆ 4   │
│ C    ┆ 5   │
│ null ┆ 6   │
│ B    ┆ 2   │
└──────┴─────┘
count​
def count() -> TableFrame

View source ↗

len​
def len() -> TableFrame

View source ↗

Aggregation operation that counts the rows in the group.

Examples​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┬──────┐
│ ss   ┆ u    ┆ ff   │
│ ---  ┆ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  ┆ f64  │
╞══════╪══════╪══════╡
│ A    ┆ 1    ┆ 1.1  │
│ B    ┆ 0    ┆ 0.0  │
│ A    ┆ 2    ┆ 2.2  │
│ B    ┆ 3    ┆ 3.3  │
│ B    ┆ 4    ┆ 4.4  │
│ C    ┆ 5    ┆ -1.1 │
│ C    ┆ 6    ┆ -2.2 │
│ C    ┆ 7    ┆ -3.3 │
│ D    ┆ 8    ┆ inf  │
│ F    ┆ 9    ┆ NaN  │
│ null ┆ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.group_by(td.col("ss")).len()
>>>
┌──────┬─────┐
│ ss   ┆ len │
│ ---  ┆ --- │
│ str  ┆ u32 │
╞══════╪═════╡
│ null ┆ 1   │
│ B    ┆ 3   │
│ F    ┆ 1   │
│ C    ┆ 3   │
│ A    ┆ 2   │
│ D    ┆ 1   │
└──────┴─────┘
max​
def max() -> TableFrame

View source ↗

Aggregation operation that computes the maximum value in the group for of all the non group by columns.

Examples​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┬──────┐
│ ss   ┆ u    ┆ ff   │
│ ---  ┆ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  ┆ f64  │
╞══════╪══════╪══════╡
│ A    ┆ 1    ┆ 1.1  │
│ B    ┆ 0    ┆ 0.0  │
│ A    ┆ 2    ┆ 2.2  │
│ B    ┆ 3    ┆ 3.3  │
│ B    ┆ 4    ┆ 4.4  │
│ C    ┆ 5    ┆ -1.1 │
│ C    ┆ 6    ┆ -2.2 │
│ C    ┆ 7    ┆ -3.3 │
│ D    ┆ 8    ┆ inf  │
│ F    ┆ 9    ┆ NaN  │
│ null ┆ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.group_by("ss").max()
>>>
┌──────┬──────┬──────┐
│ ss   ┆ u    ┆ ff   │
│ ---  ┆ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  ┆ f64  │
╞══════╪══════╪══════╡
│ null ┆ null ┆ null │
│ A    ┆ 2    ┆ 2.2  │
│ B    ┆ 4    ┆ 4.4  │
│ C    ┆ 7    ┆ -1.1 │
│ D    ┆ 8    ┆ inf  │
│ F    ┆ 9    ┆ NaN  │
└──────┴──────┴──────┘
mean​
def mean() -> TableFrame

View source ↗

Aggregation operation that computes the mean value in the group for of all the non group by columns.

Examples​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┬──────┐
│ ss   ┆ u    ┆ ff   │
│ ---  ┆ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  ┆ f64  │
╞══════╪══════╪══════╡
│ A    ┆ 1    ┆ 1.1  │
│ B    ┆ 0    ┆ 0.0  │
│ A    ┆ 2    ┆ 2.2  │
│ B    ┆ 3    ┆ 3.3  │
│ B    ┆ 4    ┆ 4.4  │
│ C    ┆ 5    ┆ -1.1 │
│ C    ┆ 6    ┆ -2.2 │
│ C    ┆ 7    ┆ -3.3 │
│ D    ┆ 8    ┆ inf  │
│ F    ┆ 9    ┆ NaN  │
│ null ┆ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.group_by("ss").mean()
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┬──────────┐
│ ss   ┆ u        ┆ ff       │
│ ---  ┆ ---      ┆ ---      │
│ str  ┆ f64      ┆ f64      │
╞══════╪══════════╪══════════╡
│ null ┆ null     ┆ null     │
│ A    ┆ 1.5      ┆ 1.65     │
│ B    ┆ 2.333333 ┆ 2.566667 │
│ C    ┆ 6.0      ┆ -2.2     │
│ D    ┆ 8.0      ┆ inf      │
│ F    ┆ 9.0      ┆ NaN      │
└──────┴──────────┴──────────┘
median​
def median() -> TableFrame

View source ↗

Aggregation operation that computes the median value in the group for of all the non group by columns.

Examples​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┬──────┐
│ ss   ┆ u    ┆ ff   │
│ ---  ┆ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  ┆ f64  │
╞══════╪══════╪══════╡
│ A    ┆ 1    ┆ 1.1  │
│ B    ┆ 0    ┆ 0.0  │
│ A    ┆ 2    ┆ 2.2  │
│ B    ┆ 3    ┆ 3.3  │
│ B    ┆ 4    ┆ 4.4  │
│ C    ┆ 5    ┆ -1.1 │
│ C    ┆ 6    ┆ -2.2 │
│ C    ┆ 7    ┆ -3.3 │
│ D    ┆ 8    ┆ inf  │
│ F    ┆ 9    ┆ NaN  │
│ null ┆ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.group_by("ss").median()
>>>
┌──────┬──────┬──────┐
│ ss   ┆ u    ┆ ff   │
│ ---  ┆ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ f64  ┆ f64  │
╞══════╪══════╪══════╡
│ null ┆ null ┆ null │
│ A    ┆ 1.5  ┆ 1.65 │
│ B    ┆ 3.0  ┆ 3.3  │
│ C    ┆ 6.0  ┆ -2.2 │
│ D    ┆ 8.0  ┆ inf  │
│ F    ┆ 9.0  ┆ NaN  │
└──────┴──────┴──────┘
min​
def min() -> TableFrame

View source ↗

Aggregation operation that computes the minimum value in the group for of all the non group by columns.

Examples​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┬──────┐
│ ss   ┆ u    ┆ ff   │
│ ---  ┆ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  ┆ f64  │
╞══════╪══════╪══════╡
│ A    ┆ 1    ┆ 1.1  │
│ B    ┆ 0    ┆ 0.0  │
│ A    ┆ 2    ┆ 2.2  │
│ B    ┆ 3    ┆ 3.3  │
│ B    ┆ 4    ┆ 4.4  │
│ C    ┆ 5    ┆ -1.1 │
│ C    ┆ 6    ┆ -2.2 │
│ C    ┆ 7    ┆ -3.3 │
│ D    ┆ 8    ┆ inf  │
│ F    ┆ 9    ┆ NaN  │
│ null ┆ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.group_by("ss").min()
>>>
┌──────┬──────┬──────┐
│ ss   ┆ u    ┆ ff   │
│ ---  ┆ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  ┆ f64  │
╞══════╪══════╪══════╡
│ null ┆ null ┆ null │
│ A    ┆ 1    ┆ 1.1  │
│ B    ┆ 0    ┆ 0.0  │
│ C    ┆ 5    ┆ -3.3 │
│ D    ┆ 8    ┆ inf  │
│ F    ┆ 9    ┆ NaN  │
└──────┴──────┴──────┘
n_unique​
def n_unique() -> TableFrame

View source ↗

Aggregation operation that counts the unique values of the given column in the group for of all the non group by columns.

Examples​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┬──────┐
│ ss   ┆ u    ┆ ff   │
│ ---  ┆ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  ┆ f64  │
╞══════╪══════╪══════╡
│ A    ┆ 1    ┆ 1.1  │
│ B    ┆ 0    ┆ 0.0  │
│ A    ┆ 2    ┆ 2.2  │
│ B    ┆ 3    ┆ 3.3  │
│ B    ┆ 4    ┆ 4.4  │
│ C    ┆ 5    ┆ -1.1 │
│ C    ┆ 6    ┆ -2.2 │
│ C    ┆ 7    ┆ -3.3 │
│ D    ┆ 8    ┆ inf  │
│ F    ┆ 9    ┆ NaN  │
│ null ┆ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.group_by("ss").n_unique()
>>>
┌──────┬─────┬─────┐
│ ss   ┆ u   ┆ ff  │
│ ---  ┆ --- ┆ --- │
│ str  ┆ u32 ┆ u32 │
╞══════╪═════╪═════╡
│ null ┆ 1   ┆ 1   │
│ A    ┆ 2   ┆ 2   │
│ B    ┆ 3   ┆ 3   │
│ C    ┆ 3   ┆ 3   │
│ D    ┆ 1   ┆ 1   │
│ F    ┆ 1   ┆ 1   │
└──────┴─────┴─────┘
sum​
def sum() -> TableFrame

View source ↗

Aggregation operation that computes the sum for all values in the group for of all the non group by columns.

Examples​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┬──────┐
│ ss   ┆ u    ┆ ff   │
│ ---  ┆ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  ┆ f64  │
╞══════╪══════╪══════╡
│ A    ┆ 1    ┆ 1.1  │
│ B    ┆ 0    ┆ 0.0  │
│ A    ┆ 2    ┆ 2.2  │
│ B    ┆ 3    ┆ 3.3  │
│ B    ┆ 4    ┆ 4.4  │
│ C    ┆ 5    ┆ -1.1 │
│ C    ┆ 6    ┆ -2.2 │
│ C    ┆ 7    ┆ -3.3 │
│ D    ┆ 8    ┆ inf  │
│ F    ┆ 9    ┆ NaN  │
│ null ┆ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.group_by("ss").sum()
>>>
┌──────┬─────┬──────┐
│ ss   ┆ u   ┆ ff   │
│ ---  ┆ --- ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64 ┆ f64  │
╞══════╪═════╪══════╡
│ null ┆ 0   ┆ 0.0  │
│ A    ┆ 3   ┆ 3.3  │
│ B    ┆ 7   ┆ 7.7  │
│ C    ┆ 18  ┆ -6.6 │
│ D    ┆ 8   ┆ inf  │
│ F    ┆ 9   ┆ NaN  │
└──────┴─────┴──────┘

col, lit & concat​

Expressions​

Expr
class Expr(
    expr: Expr | Expr,
)

Bases: object

abs​
def abs() -> Expr

View source ↗

Return the abso lute value of the expression.

add​
def add(
    other: Any,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Equivalent to the + operator.

For numeric types adds the given input. For string types concatenates the given input.

Parameters:

otherAnyrequired

The value to add or concatenate.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").add(1).alias("add"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ val  ┆ add      │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ i64  ┆ i64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 1    ┆ 2        │
│ 15   ┆ 16       │
│ 18   ┆ 19       │
│ 60   ┆ 61       │
│ 60   ┆ 61       │
│ 75   ┆ 76       │
│ null ┆ null     │
└──────┴──────────┘
alias​
def alias(
    name: str,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Set the name for a column or expression.

Parameters:

namestrrequired

Column or expression new name. The name must be a word ([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*) of up to 100 characters.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("age"), td.col("age").alias("Age"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ age  ┆ Age  │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ i64  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ 1    ┆ 1    │
│ 15   ┆ 15   │
│ 18   ┆ 18   │
│ 60   ┆ 60   │
│ 60   ┆ 60   │
│ 75   ┆ 75   │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
and_​
def and_(
    * others: Any,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Bitwise and operator with the given expressions. It can be used with integer and bool types.

Parameters:

othersrequired

expressions to peform the bitwiseand with.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("i"), td.col("i").and_(2).alias("and_"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ i    ┆ and_ │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ i64  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ -1   ┆ 2    │
│ 2    ┆ 2    │
│ -3   ┆ 0    │
│ 0    ┆ 0    │
│ 5    ┆ 0    │
│ 7    ┆ 2    │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
arccos​
def arccos() -> Expr

View source ↗

Calculate the inverse cosine of the element value.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").arccos().alias("arccos"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ val  ┆ arccos   │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ f64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 0.01 ┆ 1.560796 │
│ 0.15 ┆ 1.420228 │
│ 0.18 ┆ 1.38981  │
│ 0.6  ┆ 0.927295 │
│ 0.6  ┆ 0.927295 │
│ 0.75 ┆ 0.722734 │
│ null ┆ null     │
└──────┴──────────┘
arccosh​
def arccosh() -> Expr

View source ↗

Calculate the inverse hyperbolic cosine of the element value.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").arccosh().alias("arccosh"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ val  ┆ arccosh  │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ f64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 0.1  ┆ NaN      │
│ 1.5  ┆ 0.962424 │
│ 1.8  ┆ 1.192911 │
│ 6.0  ┆ 2.477889 │
│ 6.0  ┆ 2.477889 │
│ 7.5  ┆ 2.703576 │
│ null ┆ null     │
└──────┴──────────┘
arcsin​
def arcsin() -> Expr

View source ↗

Calculate the inverse sine of the element value.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").arcsin().alias("arcsin"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ val  ┆ arcsin   │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ f64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 0.01 ┆ 0.01     │
│ 0.15 ┆ 0.150568 │
│ 0.18 ┆ 0.180986 │
│ 0.6  ┆ 0.643501 │
│ 0.6  ┆ 0.643501 │
│ 0.75 ┆ 0.848062 │
│ null ┆ null     │
└──────┴──────────┘
arcsinh​
def arcsinh() -> Expr

View source ↗

Calculate the inverse hyperbolic sine of the element value.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").arcsinh().alias("arcsinh"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ val  ┆ arcsinh  │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ f64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 0.1  ┆ 0.099834 │
│ 1.5  ┆ 1.194763 │
│ 1.8  ┆ 1.350441 │
│ 6.0  ┆ 2.49178  │
│ 6.0  ┆ 2.49178  │
│ 7.5  ┆ 2.712465 │
│ null ┆ null     │
└──────┴──────────┘
arctan​
def arctan() -> Expr

View source ↗

Calculate the inverse tangent of the element value.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), , td.col("val").arctan().alias("arctan"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ val  ┆ arctan   │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ f64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 0.01 ┆ 0.01     │
│ 0.15 ┆ 0.14889  │
│ 0.18 ┆ 0.178093 │
│ 0.6  ┆ 0.54042  │
│ 0.6  ┆ 0.54042  │
│ 0.75 ┆ 0.643501 │
│ null ┆ null     │
└──────┴──────────┘
arctanh​
def arctanh() -> Expr

View source ↗

Calculate the inverse hyperbolic tangent of the element value.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), , td.col("val").arctanh().alias("arctanh"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ val  ┆ arctanh  │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ f64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 0.01 ┆ 0.01     │
│ 0.15 ┆ 0.15114  │
│ 0.18 ┆ 0.181983 │
│ 0.6  ┆ 0.693147 │
│ 0.6  ┆ 0.693147 │
│ 0.75 ┆ 0.972955 │
│ null ┆ null     │
└──────┴──────────┘
cast​
def cast(
    dtype: td_typing.TdDataType | type [ Any ],
    *,
    strict: bool = True,
    wrap_numerical: bool = False,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Cast a value to d different type.

Parameters:

dtypetd_typing.TdDataType | type [ Any ]required

The data type to cast to.

strictbooldefault: True

If false, invalid casts produce null’s; if true, an excetion is raised.

wrap_numericalbooldefault: False

If true, overflowing numbers ara handled; if false, an excetion is raised.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>> import polars as pl
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").cast(pl.Float64)).alias("cast")
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ val  ┆ cast │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ i64  ┆ f64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ 1    ┆ 1.0  │
│ 15   ┆ 15.0 │
│ 18   ┆ 18.0 │
│ 60   ┆ 60.0 │
│ 60   ┆ 60.0 │
│ 75   ┆ 75.0 │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
cbrt​
def cbrt() -> Expr

View source ↗

Calculate the cub root of the element value.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").cbrt().alias("cbrt"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ val  ┆ cbrt     │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ i64  ┆ f64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 1    ┆ 1.0      │
│ 15   ┆ 2.466212 │
│ 18   ┆ 2.620741 │
│ 60   ┆ 3.914868 │
│ 60   ┆ 3.914868 │
│ 75   ┆ 4.217163 │
│ null ┆ null     │
└──────┴──────────┘
ceil​
def ceil() -> Expr

View source ↗

Round up the expression to the next integer value.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("temp"), td.col("temp").first().ceil().alias("ceil"))
>>>
┌──────┬─────────┐
│ temp ┆ ceil    │
│ ---  ┆ ------- │
│ f64  ┆ f64     │
╞══════╪═════════╡
│ 1.0  ┆  1.0    │
│ 1.1  ┆  2.0    │
└──────┴─────────┘
clip​
def clip(
    lower_bound: NumericLiteral | TemporalLiteral | IntoExprColumn | None = None,
    upper_bound: NumericLiteral | TemporalLiteral | IntoExprColumn | None = None,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

For element values outside the lower and upper bounds, lower values are replaced with the lower bound and upper values with the upper bound. Values within the lower and upper bounds are unaffected.

Parameters:

lower_boundNumericLiteral | TemporalLiteral | IntoExprColumn | Nonedefault: None

The lower bound value. If None, the lower bound is not set.

upper_boundNumericLiteral | TemporalLiteral | IntoExprColumn | Nonedefault: None

The upper bound value. If None, the upper bound is

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("age"), td.col("age").clip(18,65).alias("clip"))
>>>
┌──────┬─────────┐
│ age  ┆ clip    │
│ ---  ┆ ------- │
│ i64  ┆ i64     │
╞══════╪═══+++═══╡
│ 1    ┆ 18      │
│ 18   ┆ 18      │
│ 50   ┆ 50      │
│ 65   ┆ 65      │
│ 70   ┆ 65      │
└──────┴─────────┘
cos​
def cos() -> Expr

View source ↗

Calculate the cosine of the element value.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").cos().alias("cos"))
>>>
┌──────┬───────────┐
│ val  ┆ cos       │
│ ---  ┆ ---       │
│ f64  ┆ f64       │
╞══════╪═══════════╡
│ 0.1  ┆ 0.995004  │
│ 1.5  ┆ 0.070737  │
│ 1.8  ┆ -0.227202 │
│ 6.0  ┆ 0.96017   │
│ 6.0  ┆ 0.96017   │
│ 7.5  ┆ 0.346635  │
│ null ┆ null      │
└──────┴───────────┘
cosh​
def cosh() -> Expr

View source ↗

Calculate the hyperbolic cosine of the element value.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").cosh().alias("cosh"))
>>>
┌──────┬────────────┐
│ val  ┆ cosh       │
│ ---  ┆ ---        │
│ f64  ┆ f64        │
╞══════╪════════════╡
│ 0.1  ┆ 1.005004   │
│ 1.5  ┆ 2.35241    │
│ 1.8  ┆ 3.107473   │
│ 6.0  ┆ 201.715636 │
│ 6.0  ┆ 201.715636 │
│ 7.5  ┆ 904.021484 │
│ null ┆ null       │
└──────┴────────────┘
cot​
def cot() -> Expr

View source ↗

Calculate the cotangent of the element value.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").cot().alias("cot"))
>>>
┌──────┬───────────┐
│ val  ┆ cot       │
│ ---  ┆ ---       │
│ f64  ┆ f64       │
╞══════╪═══════════╡
│ 0.1  ┆ 9.966644  │
│ 1.5  ┆ 0.070915  │
│ 1.8  ┆ -0.233304 │
│ 6.0  ┆ -3.436353 │
│ 6.0  ┆ -3.436353 │
│ 7.5  ┆ 0.369547  │
│ null ┆ null      │
└──────┴───────────┘
count​
def count() -> Expr

View source ↗

Aggregation operation that counts the non null values of the given column in the group.

Examples​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ a    ┆ b    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ A    ┆ 1    │
│ B    ┆ 2    │
│ A    ┆ 3    │
│ B    ┆ 0    │
│ C    ┆ 5    │
│ null ┆ 6    │
│ C    ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.group_by(td.col("a")).agg(td.col("b")).count().alias("count"))
>>>
┌──────┬───────┐
│ a    ┆ count │
│ ---  ┆ ---   │
│ str  ┆ u32   │
╞══════╪═══════╡
│ null ┆ 1     │
│ A    ┆ 2     │
│ B    ┆ 2     │
│ C    ┆ 1     │
└──────┴───────┘
degrees​
def degrees() -> Expr

View source ↗

Convert a radian value to degrees

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").degrees().alias("degrees"))
>>>
┌──────┬────────────┐
│ val  ┆ degress    │
│ ---  ┆ ---        │
│ f64  ┆ f64        │
╞══════╪════════════╡
│ 0.1  ┆ 5.729578   │
│ 1.5  ┆ 85.943669  │
│ 1.8  ┆ 103.132403 │
│ 6.0  ┆ 343.774677 │
│ 6.0  ┆ 343.774677 │
│ 7.5  ┆ 429.718346 │
│ null ┆ null       │
└──────┴────────────┘
diff​
def diff(
    n: int = 1,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Compute the difference between an element value and the element value of the specified relative row.

It supports numberic and datetime types.

Parameters:

nintdefault: 1

The relative row to compute the difference with. Defaults to 1 (previous). Use a negative number to get a next row.

Examples​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("i"), td.col("i").diff().diff().alias("diff"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ i    ┆ diff │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ i64  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ 1    ┆ null │
│ 0    ┆ -1   │
│ 2    ┆ 2    │
│ 3    ┆ 1    │
│ 4    ┆ 1    │
│ -1   ┆ -5   │
│ -2   ┆ -1   │
│ -3   ┆ -1   │
│ -4   ┆ -1   │
│ -5   ┆ -1   │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
eq​
def eq(
    other: Any,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Compare if 2 expressions are equal, equivalent to expr == other. If one of the expressions is null (None) it returns null.

Parameters:

otherAnyrequired

The value to compare with.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("a").eq(td.col("b")).alias("eq"))
>>>
┌─────┬──────┬───────┐
│ a   ┆ b    ┆ eq    │
│ --- ┆ ---  ┆ ---   │
│ f64 ┆ f64  ┆ bool  │
╞═════╪══════╪═══════╡
│ 1.0 ┆ 2.0  ┆ false │
│ 2.0 ┆ 2.0  ┆ true  │
│ NaN ┆ NaN  ┆ true  │
│ 4.0 ┆ NaN  ┆ false │
│ 5.0 ┆ null ┆ null  │
│ null┆ null ┆ null  │
└─────┴──────┴───────┘
eq_missing​
def eq_missing(
    other: Any,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Compare if 2 expressions are equal an, equivalent to expr == other. If one of the expressions is null (None) it returns false.

Parameters:

otherAnyrequired

The value to compare with.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("a").eq(td.col("b")).alias("eq_missing"))
>>>
┌─────┬──────┬───────────┐
│ a   ┆ b    ┆ eq_missing│
│ --- ┆ ---  ┆ ---       │
│ f64 ┆ f64  ┆ bool      │
╞═════╪══════╪═══════════╡
│ 1.0 ┆ 2.0  ┆ false     │
│ 2.0 ┆ 2.0  ┆ true      │
│ NaN ┆ NaN  ┆ true      │
│ 4.0 ┆ NaN  ┆ false     │
│ 5.0 ┆ null ┆ false     │
│ null┆ null ┆ true      │
└─────┴──────┴───────────┘
exp​
def exp() -> Expr

View source ↗

Calculate the exponential of the element value.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").exp().alias("exp"))
>>>
┌──────┬─────────────┐
│ val  ┆ exp         │
│ ---  ┆ ---         │
│ f64  ┆ f64         │
╞══════╪═════════════╡
│ 0.1  ┆ 1.105171    │
│ 6.0  ┆ 403.428793  │
│ 6.0  ┆ 403.428793  │
│ 7.5  ┆ 1808.042414 │
│ null ┆ null        │
└──────┴─────────────┘
fill_nan​
def fill_nan(
    value: int | float | Expr | None,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Replace NaN values with the given value.

Parameters:

valueint | float | Expr | Nonerequired

The value to replaceNaN values with.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").fill_nan(5.5)
>>>        .alias("fill_nan"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ val  ┆ fill_nan │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ f64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 1.1  ┆ 1.1      │
│ 2.0  ┆ 2.0      │
│ inf  ┆ inf      │
│ null ┆ null     │
│ NaN  ┆ 5.5      │
└──────┴──────────┘
fill_null​
def fill_null(
    value: Any | Expr | None = None,
    strategy: Literal [ 'forward' , 'backward' , 'min' , 'max' , 'mean' , 'zero' , 'one' ] | None = None,
    limit: int | None = None,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Replace null values with the given value.

Parameters:

valueAny | Expr | Nonedefault: None

The value to replacenull values with.

strategyLiteral [ 'forward' , 'backward' , 'min' , 'max' , 'mean' , 'zero' , 'one' ] | Nonedefault: None

The strategy to use for fillingnull values.

limitint | Nonedefault: None

The maximum number ofnull values to replace.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val")
>>>        .fill_null(5.5).alias("fill_null"))
>>>
┌──────┬───────────┐
│ val  ┆ fill_null │
│ ---  ┆ ---       │
│ f64  ┆ f64       │
╞══════╪═══════════╡
│ -1.0 ┆ -1.0      │
│ 0.0  ┆ 0.0       │
│ 1.1  ┆ 1.1       │
│ 2.0  ┆ 2.0       │
│ inf  ┆ inf       │
│ null ┆ 5.5       │
│ NaN  ┆ NaN       │
└──────┴───────────┘
filter​
def filter(
    * predicates: Expr | Series | str | Iterable [ Expr | Series | str ],
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Apply a filter predicate to an expression.

Elements for which the predicate does not evaluate to true are discarded, evaluations to null are also discarded.

Useful in an aggregation expression.

Parameters:

predicatesrequired

Expression(s) that evaluates to a boolean.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌───────┬─────────┐
│ state ┆ tickets │
│ ---   ┆ ---     │
│ str   ┆ i64     │
╞═══════╪═════════╡
│ CA    ┆ 1       │
│ AL    ┆ 3       │
│ CA    ┆ 2       │
│ NY    ┆ 2       │
│ NY    ┆ 3       │
└───────┴─────────┘
>>>
>>> import tabsdata as td
>>> tf.group_by("state").agg(td.col("tickets")
>>>   .filter(td.col("tickets") !=2)
>>>   .sum().alias("sum_non_two"))
>>>
┌───────┬─────────────┐
│ state ┆ sum_non_two │
│ ---   ┆ ---         │
│ str   ┆ i64         │
╞═══════╪═════════════╡
│ AL    ┆ 3           │
│ NY    ┆ 3           │
│ CA    ┆ 1           │
└───────┴─────────────┘
first​
def first() -> Expr

View source ↗

Get the first element.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("age"), td.col("age").first().alias("first"))
>>>
┌──────┬─────────┐
│ age  ┆ first   │
│ ---  ┆ ------- │
│ i64  ┆ i64     │
╞══════╪═════════╡
│ 10   ┆ 10      │
│ 11   ┆ 10      │
│ 18   ┆ 10      │
│ 65   ┆ 10      │
│ 70   ┆ 10      │
└──────┴─────────┘
floor​
def floor() -> Expr

View source ↗

Round down the expression to the previous integer value.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("temp"), td.col("temp").ceil().alias("floor"))
>>>
┌──────┬─────────┐
│ temp ┆ floor   │
│ ---  ┆ ------- │
│ f64  ┆ i64     │
╞══════╪═════════╡
│ 1.0  ┆  1      │
│ 1.1  ┆  1      │
└──────┴─────────┘
floordiv​
def floordiv(
    other: Any,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Calculate the floor on the division.

Parameters:

otherAnyrequired

The value to divide by.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("temp"), td.col("temp").floordiv(2)
>>>        .alias("floordiv"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ temp ┆ floordiv │
│ ---  ┆ -------  │
│ f64  ┆ i64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 2.5  ┆  1       │
│ 1.4  ┆  0       │
└──────┴──────────┘
ge​
def ge(
    other: Any,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Greater or equal operator.

Parameters:

otherAnyrequired

The value to compare with.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("temp"), td.col("temp").ge(1.0).alias("ge"))
>>>
┌──────┬─────────┐
│ temp ┆ ge      │
│ ---  ┆ ------- │
│ f64  ┆ bool    │
╞══════╪═════════╡
│ 0.9  ┆  false  │
│ 1.1  ┆  true   │
└──────┴─────────┘
gt​
def gt(
    other: Any,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Greater than operator.

Parameters:

otherAnyrequired

The value to compare with.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("temp"), td.col("temp").gt(1.0).alias("gt"))
>>>
┌──────┬─────────┐
│ temp ┆ ge      │
│ ---  ┆ ------- │
│ f64  ┆ bool    │
╞══════╪═════════╡
│ 1.0  ┆  false  │
│ 1.1  ┆  true   │
└──────┴─────────┘
hash​
def hash(
    seed: int = 0,
    seed_1: int | None = None,
    seed_2: int | None = None,
    seed_3: int | None = None,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Compute the hash of an element value.

Parameters:

seedintdefault: 0

The seed for the hash function.

seed_1int | Nonedefault: None

The first seed for the hash function.

seed_2int | Nonedefault: None

The second seed for the hash function.

seed_3int | Nonedefault: None

The third seed for the hash function.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("temp"), td.col("temp").hash(5).alias("hash"))
>>>
┌──────┬─────────────────────┐
│ temp ┆ hash                │
│ ---  ┆ ---                 │
│ f64  ┆ u64                 │
╞══════╪═════════════════════╡
│ 1.1  ┆ 1438840365631752616 │
│ 2.0  ┆ 4738789230185236462 │
└──────┴─────────────────────┘
is_between​
def is_between(
    lower_bound: IntoExpr,
    upper_bound: IntoExpr,
    closed: ClosedInterval = 'both',
) -> Expr

View source ↗

If an expression is between the given bounds.

Parameters:

lower_boundIntoExprrequired

The lower bound value.

upper_boundIntoExprrequired

The upper bound value.

closedClosedIntervaldefault: 'both'

The interval type, either “both”, “left”, “right”, or “neither”

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("temp"), td.col("temp")
>>> .is_between(0, 1).alias("between"))
>>>
┌──────┬─────────┐
│ temp ┆ between │
│ ---  ┆ ------- │
│ f64  ┆ bool    │
╞══════╪═════════╡
│-1.0  ┆  false  │
│ 0.0  ┆  true   │
│ 0.5  ┆  true   │
│ 1.0  ┆  true   │
│ 1.1  ┆  false  │
└──────┴─────────┘
is_finite​
def is_finite() -> Expr

View source ↗

If an element value is finite.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("temp"), td.col("temp").is_finite()
>>>        .alias("finite"))
>>>
┌──────┬────────┐
│ temp ┆ finite │
│ ---  ┆ ---    │
│ f64  ┆ bool   │
╞══════╪════════╡
│ 1.1  ┆ true   │
│ 2.0  ┆ true   │
│ inf  ┆ false  │
└──────┴────────┘
is_in​
def is_in(
    other: Expr | Collection [ Any ] | Series,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

If an element value is in the given collection.

Parameters:

otherExpr | Collection [ Any ] | Seriesrequired

The collection to check if the element value is in.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("temp"), td.col("temp").is_in([1.1, 2.2])
>>>        .alias("is_in"))
>>>
┌──────┬────────┐
│ temp ┆ is_in  │
│ ---  ┆ ---    │
│ f64  ┆ bool   │
╞══════╪════════╡
│ 1.1  ┆ true   │
│ 2.0  ┆ false  │
│ inf  ┆ false  │
└──────┴────────┘
is_infinite​
def is_infinite() -> Expr

View source ↗

If an element value is infinite.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("temp"), td.col("temp")
>>>        .is_infinite().alias("infinite"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ temp ┆ infinite │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ bool     │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 1.1  ┆ false    │
│ 2.0  ┆ false    │
│ inf  ┆ true     │
└──────┴──────────┘
is_nan​
def is_nan() -> Expr

View source ↗

If an element value is NaN.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("temp"), td.col("temp").is_nan().alias("nan"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ temp ┆ nan      │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ bool     │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 1.1  ┆ false    │
│ Nan  ┆ true     │
│ None ┆ false    │
│ inf  ┆ false    │
└──────┴──────────┘
is_not_nan​
def is_not_nan() -> Expr

View source ↗

If an element value is not NaN.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("temp"), td.col("temp").is_not_nan()
>>>        .alias("not_nan"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ temp ┆ not_nan  │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ bool     │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 1.1  ┆ true     │
│ Nan  ┆ false    │
│ None ┆ false    │
│ inf  ┆ true     │
└──────┴──────────┘
is_not_null​
def is_not_null() -> Expr

View source ↗

If an element value is not null (None).

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("temp"), td.col("temp").is_not_null()
>>>        .alias("null"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ temp ┆ not_nulL │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ bool     │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 1.1  ┆ true     │
│ Nan  ┆ true     │
│ None ┆ false    │
│ inf  ┆ true     │
└──────┴──────────┘
is_null​
def is_null() -> Expr

View source ↗

If an element value is null (None).

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("temp"), td.col("temp").is_not_null()
>>>        .alias("null"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ temp ┆ null     │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ bool     │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 1.1  ┆ false    │
│ Nan  ┆ false    │
│ None ┆ true     │
│ inf  ┆ false    │
└──────┴──────────┘
is_unique​
def is_unique() -> Expr

View source ↗

If an element value is unique for all values in the column.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("temp"), td.col("temp").is_unique()
>>>        .alias("unique"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ temp ┆ unique   │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ bool     │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 1.1  ┆ false    │
│ 1.1  ┆ false    │
│ None ┆ true     │
│ 2.0  ┆ true     │
└──────┴──────────┘
last​
def last() -> Expr

View source ↗

Get the last element.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("age"), td.col("age").first().alias("last"))
>>>
┌──────┬─────────┐
│ age  ┆ last    │
│ ---  ┆ ------- │
│ i64  ┆ i64     │
╞══════╪═════════╡
│ 10   ┆ 70      │
│ 11   ┆ 70      │
│ 18   ┆ 70      │
│ 65   ┆ 70      │
│ 70   ┆ 70      │
└──────┴─────────┘
le​
def le(
    other: Any,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Less or equal operator.

Parameters:

otherAnyrequired

The value to compare with.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("temp"), td.col("temp").le(1.0).alias("le"))
>>>
┌──────┬─────────┐
│ temp ┆ le      │
│ ---  ┆ ------- │
│ f64  ┆ bool    │
╞══════╪═════════╡
│ 0.9  ┆  true   │
│ 1.1  ┆  false  │
└──────┴─────────┘
len​
def len() -> Expr

View source ↗

Aggregation operation that counts the rows in the group.

Examples​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ a    ┆ b    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ A    ┆ 1    │
│ B    ┆ 2    │
│ A    ┆ 3    │
│ B    ┆ 0    │
│ C    ┆ 5    │
│ null ┆ 6    │
│ C    ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.group_by(td.col("a")).agg(td.col("b")).len().alias("len"))
>>>
┌──────┬─────┐
│ a    ┆ len │
│ ---  ┆ --- │
│ str  ┆ u32 │
╞══════╪═════╡
│ null ┆ 1   │
│ A    ┆ 2   │
│ B    ┆ 2   │
│ C    ┆ 2   │
└──────┴─────┘
log​
def log(
    base: float = 2.718281828459045,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Calculate the logarithm to the given base.

Parameters:

basefloatdefault: 2.718281828459045

logarithm base, defaults toe.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").log().alias("log")
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ val  ┆ log      │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ f64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ -1.0 ┆ NaN      │
│ 0.0  ┆ -inf     │
│ 1.1  ┆ 0.09531  │
│ 2.0  ┆ 0.693147 │
│ inf  ┆ inf      │
│ null ┆ null     │
│ NaN  ┆ NaN      │
└──────┴──────────┘
log10​
def log10() -> Expr

View source ↗

Calculate the logarithm base 10.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").log10().alias("log10")
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ val  ┆ log10    │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ f64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ -1.0 ┆ NaN      │
│ 0.0  ┆ -inf     │
│ 1.1  ┆ 0.041393 │
│ 2.0  ┆ 0.30103  │
│ inf  ┆ inf      │
│ null ┆ null     │
│ NaN  ┆ NaN      │
└──────┴──────────┘
log1p​
def log1p() -> Expr

View source ↗

Calculate the natural logarithm plus one.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").log1p().alias("log1p")
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ val  ┆ log1p    │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ f64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ -1.0 ┆ -inf     │
│ 0.0  ┆ 0.0      │
│ 1.1  ┆ 0.741937 │
│ 2.0  ┆ 1.098612 │
│ inf  ┆ inf      │
│ null ┆ null     │
│ NaN  ┆ NaN      │
└──────┴──────────┘
lt​
def lt(
    other: Any,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Less than operator.

Parameters:

otherAnyrequired

The value to compare with.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").lt(1.0).alias("lt"))
>>>
┌──────┬─────────┐
│ val  ┆ tl      │
│ ---  ┆ ------- │
│ f64  ┆ bool    │
╞══════╪═════════╡
│ 1.0  ┆  false  │
│ 0.1  ┆  true   │
└──────┴─────────┘
max​
def max() -> Expr

View source ↗

Aggregation operation that finds the maximum value in the group.

Examples​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ ss   ┆ i    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ A    ┆ 1    │
│ B    ┆ 0    │
│ A    ┆ 2    │
│ B    ┆ 3    │
│ B    ┆ 4    │
│ C    ┆ -1   │
│ C    ┆ -2   │
│ C    ┆ -3   │
│ D    ┆ -4   │
│ F    ┆ -5   │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.group_by(td.col("a")).agg(td.col("b")).max())
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ ss   ┆ i    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ F    ┆ -5   │
│ C    ┆ -1   │
│ A    ┆ 2    │
│ B    ┆ 4    │
│ D    ┆ -4   │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
mean​
def mean() -> Expr

View source ↗

Aggregation operation that finds the mean of the values in the group.

Examples​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ ss   ┆ i    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ A    ┆ 1    │
│ B    ┆ 0    │
│ A    ┆ 2    │
│ B    ┆ 3    │
│ B    ┆ 4    │
│ C    ┆ -1   │
│ C    ┆ -2   │
│ C    ┆ -3   │
│ D    ┆ -4   │
│ F    ┆ -5   │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.group_by(td.col("a")).agg(td.col("b")).mean())
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ ss   ┆ i        │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ str  ┆ f64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ null ┆ null     │
│ A    ┆ 1.5      │
│ F    ┆ -5.0     │
│ C    ┆ -2.0     │
│ D    ┆ -4.0     │
│ B    ┆ 2.333333 │
└──────┴──────────┘
median​
def median() -> Expr

View source ↗

Aggregation operation that finds the median of the values in the group.

Examples​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ ss   ┆ i    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ A    ┆ 1    │
│ B    ┆ 0    │
│ A    ┆ 2    │
│ B    ┆ 3    │
│ B    ┆ 4    │
│ C    ┆ -1   │
│ C    ┆ -2   │
│ C    ┆ -3   │
│ D    ┆ -4   │
│ F    ┆ -5   │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.group_by(td.col("a")).agg(td.col("b")).median())
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ ss   ┆ i    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ f64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ F    ┆ -5.0 │
│ C    ┆ -2.0 │
│ B    ┆ 3.0  │
│ D    ┆ -4.0 │
│ A    ┆ 1.5  │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
min​
def min() -> Expr

View source ↗

Aggregation operation that finds the minimum value in the group.

Examples​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ ss   ┆ i    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ A    ┆ 1    │
│ B    ┆ 0    │
│ A    ┆ 2    │
│ B    ┆ 3    │
│ B    ┆ 4    │
│ C    ┆ -1   │
│ C    ┆ -2   │
│ C    ┆ -3   │
│ D    ┆ -4   │
│ F    ┆ -5   │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.group_by(td.col("a")).agg(td.col("b")).min())
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ ss   ┆ i    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ C    ┆ -3   │
│ A    ┆ 1    │
│ null ┆ null │
│ B    ┆ 0    │
│ F    ┆ -5   │
│ D    ┆ -4   │
└──────┴──────┘
mod​
def mod(
    other: Any,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Modulus operator.

Parameters:

otherAnyrequired

The value to divide by.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").mod(5).alias("mod"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ val  ┆ mod  │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ i64  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ 1    ┆ 1    │
│ 15   ┆ 0    │
│ 18   ┆ 3    │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
mul​
def mul(
    other: Any,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Multiplication operator.

Parameters:

otherAnyrequired

The value to multiply by.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").mul(10).alias("mul"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ val  ┆ mul  │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ i64  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ 1    ┆ 10   │
│ 15   ┆ 150  │
│ 18   ┆ 180  │
│ 75   ┆ 750  │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
n_unique​
def n_unique() -> Expr

View source ↗

Aggregation operation that counts the unique values of the given column in the group.

Examples​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ ss   ┆ i    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ A    ┆ 1    │
│ B    ┆ 0    │
│ A    ┆ 2    │
│ B    ┆ 3    │
│ B    ┆ 4    │
│ C    ┆ -1   │
│ C    ┆ -2   │
│ C    ┆ -3   │
│ D    ┆ -4   │
│ F    ┆ -5   │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.group_by(td.col("a")).agg(td.col("b")).n_unique())
>>>
┌──────┬─────┐
│ ss   ┆ i   │
│ ---  ┆ --- │
│ str  ┆ u32 │
╞══════╪═════╡
│ D    ┆ 1   │
│ C    ┆ 3   │
│ A    ┆ 2   │
│ B    ┆ 3   │
│ F    ┆ 1   │
│ null ┆ 1   │
└──────┴─────┘
ne​
def ne(
    other: Any,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Compare if 2 expressions are not equal, equivalent to expr != other. If one of the expressions is null (None) it returns null.

Parameters:

otherAnyrequired

The value to compare with.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("a").ne(td.col("b")).alias("ne"))
>>>
┌─────┬──────┬───────┐
│ a   ┆ b    ┆ ne    │
│ --- ┆ ---  ┆ ---   │
│ f64 ┆ f64  ┆ bool  │
╞═════╪══════╪═══════╡
│ 1.0 ┆ 2.0  ┆ true  │
│ 2.0 ┆ 2.0  ┆ false │
│ NaN ┆ NaN  ┆ false │
│ 4.0 ┆ NaN  ┆ true  │
│ 5.0 ┆ null ┆ null  │
│ null┆ null ┆ null  │
└─────┴──────┴───────┘
ne_missing​
def ne_missing(
    other: Any,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Compare if 2 expressions are not equal an, equivalent to expr != other. If one of the expressions is null (None) it returns false.

Parameters:

otherAnyrequired

The value to compare with.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("a").eq(td.col("b")).alias("ne_missing"))
>>>
┌─────┬──────┬───────────┐
│ a   ┆ b    ┆ ne_missing│
│ --- ┆ ---  ┆ ---       │
│ f64 ┆ f64  ┆ bool      │
╞═════╪══════╪═══════════╡
│ 1.0 ┆ 2.0  ┆ true      │
│ 2.0 ┆ 2.0  ┆ false     │
│ NaN ┆ NaN  ┆ false     │
│ 4.0 ┆ NaN  ┆ true      │
│ 5.0 ┆ null ┆ true      │
│ null┆ null ┆ false     │
└─────┴──────┴───────────┘
neg​
def neg() -> Expr

View source ↗

Unary minus operator.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").neg().alias("neg"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ val  ┆ neg  │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ f64  ┆ f64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ -1.0 ┆ 1.0  │
│ 0.0  ┆ -0.0 │
│ 1.1  ┆ -1.1 │
│ 2.0  ┆ -2.0 │
│ inf  ┆ -inf │
│ null ┆ null │
│ NaN  ┆ NaN  │
└──────┴──────┘
not_​
def not_() -> Expr

View source ↗

Negate a boolean expression.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").not_().alias("not"))
>>>
┌───────┬───────┐
│ val   ┆ not   │
│ ---   ┆ ---   │
│ bool  ┆ bool  │
╞═══════╪═══════╡
│ true  ┆ false │
│ false ┆ true  │
│ null  ┆ null  │
└───────┴───────┘
or_​
def or_(
    * others: Any,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Bitwise or operator with the given expressions. It can be used with integer and bool types.

Parameters:

othersrequired

expressions to peform the bitwiseor with.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").or_(1).alias("or"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ val  ┆ or   │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ i64  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ 1    ┆ 1    │
│ 15   ┆ 15   │
│ 18   ┆ 19   │
│ 60   ┆ 61   │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
pow​
def pow(
    exponent: Expr | Series | str | int | float,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Exponentiation operator.

Parameters:

exponentExpr | Series | str | int | floatrequired

exponent value.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").pow(2).alias("pow"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ age  ┆ pow  │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ i64  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ 1    ┆ 1    │
│ 15   ┆ 225  │
│ 18   ┆ 324  │
│ 60   ┆ 3600 │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
radians​
def radians() -> Expr

View source ↗

Convert a degree value to radians

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").radians().alias("radians"))
>>>
┌─────────┬──────────┐
│ val     ┆ radians  │
│ ---     ┆ ---      │
│ i64     ┆ f64      │
╞═════════╪══════════╡
│ 1       ┆ 0.017453 │
│ 15      ┆ 0.261799 │
│ 60      ┆ 1.047198 │
│ 75      ┆ 1.308997 │
│ null    ┆ null     │
└─────────┴──────────┘
rank​
def rank(
    method: Literal [ 'average' , 'min' , 'max' , 'dense' , 'ordinal' , 'random' ] = 'average',
    *,
    descending: bool = False,
    seed: int | None = None,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Compute the rank of the element values. Multiple rank types are available.

Parameters:

methodLiteral [ 'average' , 'min' , 'max' , 'dense' , 'ordinal' , 'random' ]default: 'average'

the ranking type: ‘average’ (default), ‘dense’, ‘max’, ‘min’, ‘ordinal’ or ‘random’.

descendingbooldefault: False

if the order is ascending (default) or descending.

seedint | Nonedefault: None

random seed when using ‘random’ rank type.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").rank("max").alias("rank"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ val  ┆ rank │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ f64  ┆ u32  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ -1.0 ┆ 1    │
│ 0.0  ┆ 2    │
│ 1.1  ┆ 3    │
│ 2.0  ┆ 4    │
│ inf  ┆ 5    │
│ null ┆ null │
│ NaN  ┆ 6    │
└──────┴──────┘
reinterpret​
def reinterpret(
    *,
    signed: bool = True,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Reinterpret the 64bit element values (i64 or u64) as a signed/unsigned integers. Only valid for 64bit integers, for other types use cast.

Parameters:

signedbooldefault: True

true to convert to i64, false to convert to u64. This named argument must be specified.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val")
>>>   .reinterpret(signed=False).alias("reinterpret"))
>>>
┌──────┬─────────────┐
│ val  ┆ reinterpret │
│ ---  ┆ ---         │
│ i64  ┆ u64         │
╞══════╪═════════════╡
│ 3    ┆ 3           │
│ 1    ┆ 1           │
│ 5    ┆ 5           │
│ 4    ┆ 4           │
│ 2    ┆ 2           │
│ 6    ┆ 6           │
│ null ┆ null        │
└──────┴─────────────┘
round​
def round(
    decimals: int = 0,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Round floating point element values.

Parameters:

decimalsintdefault: 0

number of decimal places to round to.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").round().alias("round"))
>>>
┌──────┬───────┐
│ val  ┆ round │
│ ---  ┆ ---   │
│ f64  ┆ f64   │
╞══════╪═══════╡
│ -1.0 ┆ -1.0  │
│ 0.0  ┆ 0.0   │
│ 1.1  ┆ 1.0   │
│ 2.0  ┆ 2.0   │
│ inf  ┆ inf   │
│ null ┆ null  │
│ NaN  ┆ NaN   │
└──────┴───────┘
round_sig_figs​
def round_sig_figs(
    digits: int,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Round floating point element values to the specified significant figures.

Parameters:

digitsintrequired

number of digits to round up.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").round_sig_figs(2)
>>>   .alias("round_sig_figs"))
>>>
┌────────┬────────────────┐
│ val    ┆ round_sig_figs │
│ ---    ┆ ---            │
│ f64    ┆ f64            │
╞════════╪════════════════╡
│ 0.0123 ┆ 0.012          │
│ 2.0244 ┆ 2.0            │
│ 0.0    ┆ 0.0            │
│ inf    ┆ NaN            │
│ 50.0   ┆ 50.0           │
│ 1.0    ┆ 1.0            │
│ NaN    ┆ NaN            │
│ null   ┆ null           │
│ 112.0  ┆ 110.0          │
│ 2142.0 ┆ 2100.0         │
└────────┴────────────────┘
shrink_dtype​
def shrink_dtype() -> Expr

View source ↗

Cast down a column to the smallest type that can hold the element values.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").shrink_dtype()
>>>   .alias("shrink_dtype"))
>>>
┌───────┬──────────────┐
│ val   ┆ shrink_dtype │
│ ---   ┆ ---          │
│ i64   ┆ i32          │
╞═══════╪══════════════╡
│ 0     ┆ 0            │
│ 256   ┆ 256          │
│ 65025 ┆ 65025        │
└───────┴──────────────┘
sign​
def sign() -> Expr

View source ↗

Calculate the sign of element values.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").sign().alias("sign"))
>>>
┌────────┬──────┐
│ val    ┆ sign │
│ ---    ┆ ---  │
│ f64    ┆ f64  │
╞════════╪══════╡
│ 0.0123 ┆ 1.0  │
│ 2.0244 ┆ 1.0  │
│ 0.0    ┆ 0.0  │
│ inf    ┆ 1.0  │
│ -50.0  ┆ -1.0 │
│ 1.0    ┆ 1.0  │
│ NaN    ┆ NaN  │
│ null   ┆ null │
│ -112.0 ┆ -1.0 │
│ 2142.0 ┆ 1.0  │
└────────┴──────┘
sin​
def sin() -> Expr

View source ↗

Calculate the sine of the element value.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").sin().alias("sin"))
>>>
┌─────┬───────────┐
│ val ┆ sin       │
│ --- ┆ ---       │
│ i64 ┆ f64       │
╞═════╪═══════════╡
│ 0   ┆ 0.0       │
│ 30  ┆ -0.988032 │
│ 60  ┆ -0.304811 │
│ 90  ┆ 0.893997  │
└─────┴───────────┘
sinh​
def sinh() -> Expr

View source ↗

Calculate the hyperbolic sine of the element value.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").sinh().alias("sinh"))
>>>
┌─────┬───────────┐
│ val ┆ sinh      │
│ --- ┆ ---       │
│ i64 ┆ f64       │
╞═════╪═══════════╡
│ 0   ┆ 0.0       │
│ 30  ┆ 5.3432e12 │
│ 60  ┆ 5.7100e25 │
│ 90  ┆ 6.1020e38 │
└─────┴───────────┘
slice​
def slice(
    offset: int | Expr,
    length: int | Expr | None = None,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Compute a slice of the TableFrame for the specified columns.

Parameters:

offsetint | Exprrequired

the offset to start the slice.

lengthint | Expr | Nonedefault: None

the length of the slice.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ x    ┆ y    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ f64  ┆ f64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ 1.0  ┆ 2.0  │
│ 2.0  ┆ 2.0  │
│ NaN  ┆ NaN  │
│ 4.0  ┆ NaN  │
│ 5.0  ┆ null │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.select(tf.all().slice(1,2))
>>>
┌─────┬─────┐
│ x   ┆ y   │
│ --- ┆ --- │
│ f64 ┆ f64 │
╞═════╪═════╡
│ 2.0 ┆ 2.0 │
│ NaN ┆ NaN │
└─────┴─────┘
sqrt​
def sqrt() -> Expr

View source ↗

Calculate the square root of the element value.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").sqrt().alias("sqrt"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ val  ┆ sqrt     │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ f64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 1.0  ┆ 1.0      │
│ 2.0  ┆ 1.414214 │
│ NaN  ┆ NaN      │
│ 4.0  ┆ 2.0      │
│ 5.0  ┆ 2.236068 │
│ null ┆ null     │
└──────┴──────────┘
sub​
def sub(
    other: Any,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Equivalent to the - operator.

Parameters:

otherAnyrequired

value to subtract.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").sub(1).alias("sub"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ val  ┆ sub      │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ i64  ┆ i64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 1    ┆  0       │
│ 15   ┆ 14       │
│ 18   ┆ 17       │
│ 60   ┆ 59       │
│ 60   ┆ 59       │
│ 75   ┆ 74       │
│ null ┆ null     │
└──────┴──────────┘
sum​
def sum() -> Expr

View source ↗

Aggregation operation that sums the values in the group.

Examples​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ ss   ┆ i    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ A    ┆ 1    │
│ B    ┆ 0    │
│ A    ┆ 2    │
│ B    ┆ 3    │
│ B    ┆ 4    │
│ C    ┆ -1   │
│ C    ┆ -2   │
│ C    ┆ -3   │
│ D    ┆ -4   │
│ F    ┆ -5   │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.group_by(td.col("a")).agg(td.col("b")).sum())
>>>
┌──────┬─────┐
│ ss   ┆ i   │
│ ---  ┆ --- │
│ str  ┆ i64 │
╞══════╪═════╡
│ null ┆ 0   │
│ A    ┆ 3   │
│ B    ┆ 7   │
│ C    ┆ -6  │
│ D    ┆ -4  │
│ F    ┆ -5  │
└──────┴─────┘
tan​
def tan() -> Expr

View source ↗

Calculate the tangent of the element value.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").tan().alias("tan"))
>>>
┌─────┬───────────┐
│ val ┆ tan       │
│ --- ┆ ---       │
│ i64 ┆ f64       │
╞═════╪═══════════╡
│ 0   ┆ 0.0       │
│ 30  ┆ -6.405331 │
│ 60  ┆ 0.32004   │
│ 90  ┆ -1.9952   │
└─────┴───────────┘
tanh​
def tanh() -> Expr

View source ↗

Calculate the hyperbolic tangent of the element value.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").tanh().alias("tanh"))
>>>
┌─────┬──────────┐
│ val ┆ tanh     │
│ --- ┆ ---      │
│ f64 ┆ f64      │
╞═════╪══════════╡
│ 0.0 ┆ 0.0      │
│ 3.0 ┆ 0.995055 │
│ 6.0 ┆ 0.999988 │
│ 9.0 ┆ 1.0      │
└─────┴──────────┘
truediv​
def truediv(
    other: Any,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Equivalent to the float / operator.

Parameters:

otherAnyrequired

value to divide by.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").truediv(3).alias("truediv"))
>>>
┌────────┬────────────┐
│ val    ┆ truediv    │
│ ---    ┆ ---        │
│ f64    ┆ f64        │
╞════════╪════════════╡
│ 0.0123 ┆ 0.0041     │
│ 2.0244 ┆ 0.6748     │
│ 0.0    ┆ 0.0        │
│ inf    ┆ inf        │
│ -50.0  ┆ -16.666667 │
│ 1.0    ┆ 0.333333   │
│ NaN    ┆ NaN        │
│ null   ┆ null       │
│ -112.0 ┆ -37.333333 │
│ 2142.0 ┆ 714.0      │
└────────┴────────────┘
xor​
def xor(
    other: Any,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Bitwise xor operator with the given expression. It can be used with integer and bool types.

Parameters:

otherAnyrequired

expression to peform the bitwisexor with.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").xor(8).alias("xor"))
>>>
┌─────┬─────┐
│ val ┆ xor │
│ --- ┆ --- │
│ i64 ┆ i64 │
╞═════╪═════╡
│ 0   ┆ 8   │
│ 30  ┆ 22  │
│ 60  ┆ 52  │
│ 90  ┆ 82  │
└─────┴─────┘
dtExprDateTimeNameSpace #

Return an object namespace with all date-time methods for a date-time value.

strExprStringNameSpace #

Return an object namespace with all string methods for a string value.

String​

ExprStringNameSpace
class ExprStringNameSpace(
    expr: ExprStringNameSpace,
)

View source ↗

Bases: object

contains​
def contains(
    pattern: str | Expr,
    *,
    literal: bool = False,
    strict: bool = True,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Evaluate if the string contains a pattern.

Parameters:

patternstr | Exprrequired

The pattern to search for.

literalbooldefault: False

Take the pattern as a literal string (not a regex).

strictbooldefault: True

if the given pattern is not valid regex, raise an error.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("a"), td.col("a").str.contains("ab").alias("contains"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ a    ┆ contains │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ str  ┆ bool     │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ a    ┆ false    │
│ ab   ┆ true     │
│ b    ┆ false    │
│ xaby ┆ true     │
│ null ┆ null     │
└──────┴──────────┘
contains_any​
def contains_any(
    patterns: td_expr.IntoExpr,
    *,
    ascii_case_insensitive: bool = False,
) -> td_expr.Expr

View source ↗

Evaluate if the string contains any of the given patterns.

Parameters:

patternstd_expr.IntoExprrequired

The patterns to search for.

ascii_case_insensitivebooldefault: False

If true, the search is case-insensitive.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("a"), td.col("a")
      .str.contains_any(["a", "b"]).alias("contains_any"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────────┐
│ a    ┆ contains_any │
│ ---  ┆ ---          │
│ str  ┆ bool         │
╞══════╪══════════════╡
│ abc  ┆ true         │
│ axy  ┆ true         │
│ xyb  ┆ true         │
│ xyz  ┆ false        │
│ null ┆ null         │
└──────┴──────────────┘
count_matches​
def count_matches(
    pattern: str | Expr,
    *,
    literal: bool = False,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Counts the ocurrrences of the given pattern in the string.

Parameters:

patternstr | Exprrequired

The pattern to extract.

literalbooldefault: False

Take the pattern as a literal string (not a regex).

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("a"), td.col("a")
      .str.count_matches("b.").alias("count_matches"))
>>>
┌───────────┬───────────────┐
│ a         ┆ count_matches │
│ ---       ┆ ---           │
│ str       ┆ u32           │
╞═══════════╪═══════════════╡
│ a bAb c d ┆ 2             │
│ bCbb c d  ┆ 2             │
│ bb        ┆ 1             │
│ b         ┆ 0             │
│ a         ┆ 0             │
│ null      ┆ null          │
└───────────┴───────────────┘
ends_with​
def ends_with(
    suffix: str | Expr,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Evaluate if the string ends with.

Parameters:

suffixstr | Exprrequired

The suffix to search for.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("a"), td.col("a").str.ends_with("b").alias("ends_with"))
>>>
┌──────┬───────────┐
│ a    ┆ ends_with │
│ ---  ┆ ---       │
│ str  ┆ bool      │
╞══════╪═══════════╡
│ a    ┆ false     │
│ ab   ┆ true      │
│ b    ┆ true      │
│ xaby ┆ false     │
│ null ┆ null      │
└──────┴───────────┘
extract​
def extract(
    pattern: Expr | Series | str,
    group_index: int = 1,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Extract a pattern from the string.

Parameters:

patternExpr | Series | strrequired

The pattern to extract.

group_indexintdefault: 1

The group index to extract.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("a"), td.col("a").str.extract("(b.b)", 1).alias("extract"))
>>>
┌───────────┬─────────┐
│ a         ┆ extract │
│ ---       ┆ ---     │
│ str       ┆ str     │
╞═══════════╪═════════╡
│ a bAb c d ┆ bAb     │
│ bCbb c d  ┆ bCb     │
│ bb        ┆ null    │
│ null      ┆ null    │
└───────────┴─────────┘
find​
def find(
    pattern: str | Expr,
    *,
    literal: bool = False,
    strict: bool = True,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Find the position of the first occurrence of the given pattern.

Parameters:

patternstr | Exprrequired

The pattern to search for.

literalbooldefault: False

Take the pattern as a literal string (not a regex).

strictbooldefault: True

if the given pattern is not valid regex, raise an error.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("a"), td.col("a").str.find("b").alias("find"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ a    ┆ find │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ u32  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ a    ┆ null │
│ ab   ┆ 1    │
│ b    ┆ 0    │
│ xaby ┆ 2    │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
head​
def head(
    n: int | Expr | Series | str,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Extract the start of the string up to the given length.

Parameters:

nint | Expr | Series | strrequired

The length of the head.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("a"), td.col("a").str.head(2).alias("head"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ a    ┆ head │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ str  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ abc  ┆ ab   │
│ a    ┆ a    │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
len_bytes​
def len_bytes() -> Expr

View source ↗

Return number of bytes (not chars) of a string.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("a"), td.col("a").str.len_bytes().alias("len_bytes"))
>>>
┌──────┬────────────┐
│ a    ┆ to_decimal │
│ ---  ┆ ---        │
│ str  ┆ u32        │
╞══════╪════════════╡
│ ab   ┆ 2          │
│ 再   ┆ 3          │
│ null ┆ null       │
└──────┴────────────┘
len_chars​
def len_chars() -> Expr

View source ↗

Return number of chars (not bytes) of a string.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("a"), td.col("a").str.len_chars().alias("len_chars"))
>>>
┌──────┬────────────┐
│ a    ┆ to_decimal │
│ ---  ┆ ---        │
│ str  ┆ u32        │
╞══════╪════════════╡
│ ab   ┆ 2          │
│ 再   ┆ 3          │
│ null ┆ null       │
└──────┴────────────┘
pad_end​
def pad_end(
    length: int,
    fill_char: str = ' ',
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Pad string values at the end to the given length using the given fill character.

Parameters:

lengthintrequired

The length to end pad the string to.

fill_charstrdefault: ' '

The character to use for padding.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("a"), td.col("a").str.pad_end(6, "-").alias("pad_end"))
>>>
┌────────┬─────────┐
│ a      ┆ pad_end │
│ ---    ┆ ---     │
│ str    ┆ str     │
╞════════╪═════════╡
│ abc    ┆ abc---  │
│    def ┆    def  │
│ null   ┆ null    │
└────────┴─────────┘
pad_start​
def pad_start(
    length: int,
    fill_char: str = ' ',
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Pad string values at the front to the given length using the given fill character.

Parameters:

lengthintrequired

The length to front pad the string to.

fill_charstrdefault: ' '

The character to use for padding.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("a"), td.col("a").str.pad_start(6, "-").alias("pad_start"))
>>>
┌────────┬───────────┐
│ a      ┆ pad_start │
│ ---    ┆ ---       │
│ str    ┆ str       │
╞════════╪═══════════╡
│ abc    ┆ ---abc    │
│    def ┆    def    │
│ null   ┆ null      │
└────────┴───────────┘
replace​
def replace(
    pattern: str | Expr,
    value: str | Expr,
    *,
    literal: bool = False,
    n: int = 1,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Replace the first occurence of a pattern with the given string.

Parameters:

patternstr | Exprrequired

The pattern to replace.

valuestr | Exprrequired

The value to replace the pattern with.

literalbooldefault: False

Take the pattern as a literal string (not a regex).

nintdefault: 1

Number of matches to replace.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("a"), td.col("a").str.replace("b", "X").alias("replace"))
>>>
┌───────────┬───────────┐
│ a         ┆ replace   │
│ ---       ┆ ---       │
│ str       ┆ str       │
╞═══════════╪═══════════╡
│ a bAb c d ┆ a XAb c d │
│ bCbb c d  ┆ XCbb c d  │
│ bb        ┆ Xb        │
│ b         ┆ X         │
│ a         ┆ a         │
