│ null      ┆ null      │
└───────────┴───────────┘
replace_all​
def replace_all(
    pattern: str | Expr,
    value: str | Expr,
    *,
    literal: bool = False,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Replace the all occurences of a pattern with the given string.

Parameters:

patternstr | Exprrequired

The pattern to replace.

valuestr | Exprrequired

The value to replace the pattern with.

literalbooldefault: False

Take the pattern as a literal string (not a regex).

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("a"), td.col("a").str.replace("b", "X").alias("replace"))
>>>
┌───────────┬─────────────┐
│ a         ┆ replace_all │
│ ---       ┆ ---         │
│ str       ┆ str         │
╞═══════════╪═════════════╡
│ a bAb c d ┆ a XAX c d   │
│ bCbb c d  ┆ XCXX c d    │
│ bb        ┆ XX          │
│ b         ┆ X           │
│ a         ┆ a           │
│ null      ┆ null        │
└───────────┴─────────────┘
replace_many​
def replace_many(
    patterns: td_expr.IntoExpr | Mapping[str, str], replace_with: td_expr.IntoExpr | NoDefault = <no_default>, *, ascii_case_insensitive: bool = False,
) -> td_expr.Expr

View source ↗

Replace the all occurences of any the given patterns with the given string.

Parameters:

patternstd_expr.IntoExpr | Mapping[str, str], replace_with: td_expr.IntoExpr | NoDefaultdefault: <no_default>, *, ascii_case_insensitive: bool = False

The patterns to replace.

replace_withrequired

The value to replace the pattern with.

ascii_case_insensitiverequired

If true, the search is case-insensitive.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("a"), td.col("a")
      .str.replace_many(["a", "b"], "X").alias("replace_many"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────────┐
│ a    ┆ replace_many │
│ ---  ┆ ---          │
│ str  ┆ str          │
╞══════╪══════════════╡
│ abc  ┆ XXc          │
│ axy  ┆ Xxy          │
│ xyb  ┆ xyX          │
│ xyz  ┆ xyz          │
│ null ┆ null         │
└──────┴──────────────┘
reverse​
def reverse() -> Expr

View source ↗

Reverse the string.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("a"), td.col("a").str.reverse().alias("reverse"))
>>>
┌──────┬─────────┐
│ a    ┆ reverse │
│ ---  ┆ ---     │
│ str  ┆ str     │
╞══════╪═════════╡
│ abc  ┆ cba     │
│ a    ┆ a       │
│ null ┆ null    │
└──────┴─────────┘
slice​
def slice(
    offset: int | Expr | Series | str,
    length: int | Expr | Series | str | None = None,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Extract the substring at the given offset for the given length.

Parameters:

offsetint | Expr | Series | strrequired

The offset to start the slice.

lengthint | Expr | Series | str | Nonedefault: None

The length of the slice. If None, slice until the end of the string.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("a"), td.col("a").str.slice(1,1).alias("slice"))
>>>
┌──────┬───────┐
│ a    ┆ slice │
│ ---  ┆ ---   │
│ str  ┆ str   │
╞══════╪═══════╡
│ abc  ┆ b     │
│ a    ┆       │
│ null ┆ null  │
└──────┴───────┘
starts_with​
def starts_with(
    prefix: str | Expr,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Evaluate if the string start with.

Parameters:

prefixstr | Exprrequired

The suffix to search for.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("a"), td.col("a")
      .str.starts_with("a").alias("starts_with"))
>>>
┌──────┬────────────┐
│ a    ┆ start_with │
│ ---  ┆ ---        │
│ str  ┆ bool       │
╞══════╪════════════╡
│ a    ┆ true       │
│ ab   ┆ true       │
│ b    ┆ false      │
│ xaby ┆ false      │
│ null ┆ null       │
└──────┴────────────┘
strip_chars​
def strip_chars(
    characters: td_expr.IntoExpr = None,
) -> td_expr.Expr

View source ↗

Trim string values.

Parameters:

characterstd_expr.IntoExprdefault: None

Characters to trim from start and end of the string. All characteres in the given string are removed, regardless the order. Default is whitespace.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("a"), td.col("a")
      .str.strip_chars("a ").alias("strip_chars"))
>>>
┌─────────────────────────────────┬─────────────┐
│ a                               ┆ strip_chars │
│ ---                             ┆ ---         │
│ str                             ┆ str         │
╞═════════════════════════════════╪═════════════╡
│ acba cda                      … ┆ cba cd      │
│    xy z                         ┆ xy z        │
│ null                            ┆ null        │
└─────────────────────────────────┴─────────────┘
strip_chars_end​
def strip_chars_end(
    characters: td_expr.IntoExpr = None,
) -> td_expr.Expr

View source ↗

Trim string values from the end of the string.

Parameters:

characterstd_expr.IntoExprdefault: None

Characters to trim from start of the string. All ending characteres in the given string are removed, regardless the order. Default is whitespace.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("a"), td.col("a")
      .str.strip_chars_end("dc ").alias("strip_chars_end"))
>>>
┌───────────────────────────────┬─────────────────┐
│ a                             ┆ strip_chars_end │
│ ---                           ┆ ---             │
│ str                           ┆ str             │
╞═══════════════════════════════╪═════════════════╡
│ cba cd                        ┆ cba             │
│    xy z                       ┆    xy z         │
│ null                          ┆ null            │
└───────────────────────────────┴─────────────────┘
strip_chars_start​
def strip_chars_start(
    characters: td_expr.IntoExpr = None,
) -> td_expr.Expr

View source ↗

Trim string values from the start of the string.

Parameters:

characterstd_expr.IntoExprdefault: None

Characters to trim from start of the string. All starting characteres in the given string are removed, regardless the order. Default is whitespace.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("a"), td.col("a")
      .str.strip_chars_start("abc").alias("strip_chars_start"))
>>>
┌───────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────┐
│ a                             ┆ strip_chars_start          │
│ ---                           ┆ ---                        │
│ str                           ┆ str                        │
╞═══════════════════════════════╪════════════════════════════╡
│ cba cd                        ┆  cd                        │
│    xy z                       ┆    xy z                    │
│ null                          ┆ null                       │
└───────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────┘
strip_prefix​
def strip_prefix(
    prefix: td_expr.IntoExpr,
) -> td_expr.Expr

View source ↗

Trim string values removing the given prefix

Parameters:

prefixtd_expr.IntoExprrequired

Prefix to remove from the string.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("a"), td.col("a")
      .str.strip_prefix("cb").alias("strip_prefix"))
>>>
┌───────────────────────────────┬─────────────────┐
│ a                             ┆ strip_prefix    │
│ ---                           ┆ ---             │
│ str                           ┆ str             │
╞═══════════════════════════════╪═════════════════╡
│ cba cd                        ┆ a cd            │
│ bx                            ┆ bx              │
│ null                          ┆ null            │
└───────────────────────────────┴─────────────────┘
strip_suffix​
def strip_suffix(
    suffix: td_expr.IntoExpr,
) -> td_expr.Expr

View source ↗

Trim string values removing the given suffix

Parameters:

suffixtd_expr.IntoExprrequired

Suffix to remove from the string.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("a"), td.col("a")
      .str.strip_suffix("cd").alias("strip_suffix"))
>>>
┌───────────────────────────────┬─────────────────┐
│ a                             ┆ strip_suffix    │
│ ---                           ┆ ---             │
│ str                           ┆ str             │
╞═══════════════════════════════╪═════════════════╡
│ cba cd                        ┆ cba             │
│ bx                            ┆ bx              │
│ null                          ┆ null            │
└───────────────────────────────┴─────────────────┘
tail​
def tail(
    n: int | Expr | Series | str,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Extract the end of the string up to the given length.

Parameters:

nint | Expr | Series | strrequired

The length of the tail.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("a"), td.col("a").str.tail(2).alias("tail"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ a    ┆ tail │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ str  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ abc  ┆ bc   │
│ a    ┆ a    │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
to_date​
def to_date(
    fmt: str | None = None,
    *,
    strict: bool = True,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Convert the string to a date.

Parameters:

fmtstr | Nonedefault: None

The date format string (default %Y-%m-%d)[formats] (https://docs.rs/chrono/0.4.19/chrono/format/strftime/index.html).

strictbooldefault: True

Whether to parse the date strictly.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("a"), td.col("a").str.to_date().alias("to_date"))
>>>
┌────────────┬────────────┐
│ a          ┆ to_date    │
│ ---        ┆ ---        │
│ str        ┆ date       │
╞════════════╪════════════╡
│ 2024-12-13 ┆ 2024-12-13 │
│ 2024-12-15 ┆ 2024-12-15 │
│ null       ┆ null       │
└────────────┴────────────┘
to_datetime​
def to_datetime(
    fmt: str | None = None,
    *,
    time_unit: Literal [ 'ns' , 'us' , 'ms' ] | None = None,
    time_zone: str | None = None,
    strict: bool = True,
    ambiguous: Literal [ 'earliest' , 'latest' , 'raise' , 'null' ] | Expr = 'raise',
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Convert the string to a datetime.

Parameters:

fmtstr | Nonedefault: None

The datetime format string (default %Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S)[formats] (https://docs.rs/chrono/0.4.19/chrono/format/strftime/index.html).

time_unitLiteral [ 'ns' , 'us' , 'ms' ] | Nonedefault: None

{None, ‘us’, ‘ns’, ‘ms’} If None (default), it inferred from the format string

time_zonestr | Nonedefault: None

Time zone for the resulting value.

strictbooldefault: True

If the conversion fails an error will be raised.

ambiguousLiteral [ 'earliest' , 'latest' , 'raise' , 'null' ] | Exprdefault: 'raise'

Policy to apply on ambiguos Datetimes: ‘raise’: saises an error ‘earliest’: use the earliest datetime ‘latest’: use the latest datetime ‘null’: set to null

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("a"), td.col("a").str.to_datetime().alias("to_datetime"))
>>>
┌─────────────────────┬─────────────────────┐
│ a                   ┆ to_datetime         │
│ ---                 ┆ ---                 │
│ str                 ┆ datetime[μs]        │
╞═════════════════════╪═════════════════════╡
│ 2024-12-13 08:45:34 ┆ 2024-12-13 08:45:34 │
│ 2024-12-15 18:33:00 ┆ 2024-12-15 18:33:00 │
│ null                ┆ null                │
└─────────────────────┴─────────────────────┘
to_integer​
def to_integer(
    *,
    base: int | Expr | Series | str = 10,
    strict: bool = True,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Covert a string to integer.

Parameters:

baseint | Expr | Series | strdefault: 10

The base of the integer.

strictbooldefault: True

If true, raise an error if the string is not a valid integer.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("a"), td.col("a")
      .str.to_integer(strict=False).alias("to_integer"))
>>>
┌──────┬────────────┐
│ a    ┆ to_integer │
│ ---  ┆ ---        │
│ str  ┆ i64        │
╞══════╪════════════╡
│ 1    ┆ 1          │
│ 2.2  ┆ null       │
│ a    ┆ null       │
│ null ┆ null       │
└──────┴────────────┘
to_lowercase​
def to_lowercase() -> Expr

View source ↗

Return the lowercase of a string.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("a"), td.col("a").str.to_lowercase().alias("to_lowercase"))
>>>
┌──────┬───────────────┐
│ a    ┆ to_lowerrcase │
│ ---  ┆ ---           │
│ str  ┆ u32           │
╞══════╪═══════════════╡
│ aB   ┆ ab            │
│ null ┆ null          │
└──────┴───────────────┘
to_time​
def to_time(
    fmt: str | None = None,
    *,
    strict: bool = True,
    cache: bool = True,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Convert the string to a time.

Parameters:

fmtstr | Nonedefault: None

The time format string (default %H:%M:%S)[formats] (https://docs.rs/chrono/0.4.19/chrono/format/strftime/index.html).

strictbooldefault: True

Whether to parse the date strictly.

cachebooldefault: True

Whether to cache the date.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("a"), td.col("a").str.to_time().alias("to_time"))
>>>
┌─────────────────────┬─────────────────────┐
│ a                   ┆ to_datetime         │
│ ---                 ┆ ---                 │
│ str                 ┆ datetime[μs]        │
╞═════════════════════╪═════════════════════╡
│ 2024-12-13 08:45:34 ┆ 2024-12-13 08:45:34 │
│ 2024-12-15 18:33:00 ┆ 2024-12-15 18:33:00 │
│ null                ┆ null                │
└─────────────────────┴─────────────────────┘
to_titlecase​
def to_titlecase() -> Expr

View source ↗

Uppercase the first character and lowercase all the others ones of a string.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("a"), td.col("a").str.to_titlecase().alias("titlecase"))
>>>
┌──────┬───────────┐
│ a    ┆ titlecase │
│ ---  ┆ ---       │
│ str  ┆ str       │
╞══════╪═══════════╡
│ ab   ┆ Ab        │
│ Ab   ┆ Ab        │
│ AB   ┆ Ab        │
│ aB   ┆ Ab        │
│ null ┆ null      │
└──────┴───────────┘
to_uppercase​
def to_uppercase() -> Expr

View source ↗

Return the uppercase of a string.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("a"), td.col("a").str.to_uppercase().alias("to_uppercase"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────────┐
│ a    ┆ to_uppercase │
│ ---  ┆ ---          │
│ str  ┆ u32          │
╞══════╪══════════════╡
│ aB   ┆ AB           │
│ null ┆ null         │
└──────┴──────────────┘
zfill​
def zfill(
    length: int | Expr | Series | str,
) -> Expr

View source ↗

Pad numeric string values at the start to the given length using zeros.

Parameters:

lengthint | Expr | Series | strrequired

The length to end pad the string to.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("a"), td.col("a").str.zfill(2).alias("zfill"))
>>>
┌──────┬───────┐
│ a    ┆ zfill │
│ ---  ┆ ---   │
│ str  ┆ str   │
╞══════╪═══════╡
│ 0    ┆ 00    │
│ 1    ┆ 01    │
│ 1000 ┆ 1000  │
│ null ┆ null  │
└──────┴───────┘
